name: Manchester
text: Amazing transport, cheap living outside city centre.
author: Student resident

name: Manchester
text: Manchester is a great city full of life and the people are lovely.
author: Student resident

name: Manchester
text: The city is student-friendly, with a good mix of shops, restaurants, and activities to do.
author: Student resident

name: Manchester
text: Manchester is basically London 2.0 so everything is mostly available here too with lesser crowds.
author: Student resident

name: London
text: London is vibrant, diverse, and never boring. However, if you are looking for the classic university experience, London definitely has a different vibe to that.
author: Student resident

name: London
text: London is the city that has it all. There's also no doubt that it is expensive, but student discounts and deals make this more manageable.
author: Student resident

name: London
text: Very expensive cost of living but I love the atmosphere and there is a lot to do around the city.
author: Student resident

name: London
text: London is one of the most expensive cities in the world, making it challenging to live here, not just for students. However, it remains my very favourite city in the world.
author: Student resident

name: London
text: Living in London as a student has been an amazing experience. The downside is definitely the cost of living — rent, transport, and even food can be expensive, so budgeting is a must.
author: Student resident

name: Hasselt University
text: Wide range of knowledge acquired and even though this comes at the price of a heavy workload, I’d say it’s worth it. The professors are very approachable and it is generally a health learning environment. For international students, the tuition is an absolute steal, given the quality of education.

name: Hasselt University
text: I chose Hasselt University for its unique and practical Master’s in Transportation Sciences, especially the focus on traffic safety. From the moment I arrived, I felt warmly welcomed and supported by the staff and the buddy program, which helped me settle in quickly. One of my favourite experiences was the World Evening event, where students from all over the world shared their cultures, traditions, and food—it truly made me feel at home and proud to represent my country. Academically, courses like the Transport Innovation Challenge gave me real-world insights and broadened my perspective. Learning Dutch through UHasselt also helped me connect with the community. Overall, Hasselt University offers a supportive and enriching environment that truly helps international students thrive.

name: Hasselt University
text: Studying Transportation Sciences at Hasselt University has been one of the most exciting and rewarding decisions I’ve made. The program pushed me to grow both academically and personally. As an international student, settling in wasn’t always easy, but making friends, both international and local, played a big role in helping me adjust. The support from the university staff especially the kindness and professionalism of Tine, Patricia, and Nadine made me feel truly seen and supported. Taking Dutch classes also helped me connect with the community and navigate daily life with more confidence. I’m deeply grateful for this journey

name: Hasselt University
text: As an Iranian student with experience in the transportation sector, I was excited to discover new aspects of the field through the courses here. Before coming to Belgium, I expected the program to be more engineering-oriented, even though I knew it was transportation sciences. Still, I really enjoyed studying in such a peaceful city. The people in Belgium are genuinely friendly and supportive, which made my experience even better.

name: Hasselt University
text: I chose Hasselt University because of the unique joint Master in Energy Engineering Technology with KU Leuven—one of the few English-taught programs in Belgium that truly bridges academic theory with hands-on industry experience. Working with real projects and cutting-edge labs like EnergyVille has been incredibly rewarding, and the professors’ close ties to the energy sector bring real-world relevance into the classroom. Adjusting to a new education system and settling into life in Belgium wasn’t always easy—language barriers and paperwork were challenging at first—but the supportive environment, approachable staff, and friendly city of Hasselt made it easier to adapt. Looking back, the mix of practical learning, strong academic support, and a welcoming international atmosphere has made this a truly valuable experience.

name: Hasselt University
text: My time at Hasselt University was truly transformative — a blend of academic growth, cultural exchange, and personal development. From the start, I was warmly welcomed and supported, which made the transition to Belgium much smoother. The international master’s program in Interior Architecture offered enriching study trips and a diverse community that encouraged cross-cultural learning. While adapting to a new academic system was challenging in the beginning, the support from professors and local classmates helped me adjust quickly. These positive experiences led me to pursue a PhD within the same department, where I found a close-knit, collaborative environment and continued opportunities for research and professional growth. UHasselt has given me lasting friendships, meaningful experiences, and a true sense of belonging.

name: Hasselt University
text: Studying the Master of Management at Hasselt University has been a great experience. I appreciate the strong balance between theoretical knowledge and real business cases, which prepares students well for real-world challenges. While learning Dutch can be difficult, the people in Hasselt are very open and supportive, and many speak English. The Dutch lessons and introduction programme offered by the university have also been very helpful in adapting to daily life in Belgium.

name: Hasselt University
text: From the first day, I felt genuinely welcomed by the academic team and the vibrant community. The support I received, particularly during the challenging times caused by the war in my home country, was profoundly impactful. The Master’s program in Transportation Sciences provided invaluable learning opportunities through hands-on, real-life projects. While all classes were conducted entirely in English, I also had the chance to enhance my Dutch skills through daily interactions. Hasselt University, combined with the charm of the city itself, offers an ideal environment for an enriching and fulfilling student experience. My journey here has been both seamless and deeply rewarding.

name: Hasselt University
text: Hasselt University is an exceptional choice for science and engineering students. The relatively small size of the university allows for personalized supervision, which facilitates interaction between students and professors. The laboratories are well equipped, and the courses are often oriented towards practical applications, which is very enriching. However, the organization of the courses could be more flexible to adapt to the needs of international students. The city of Hasselt is charming and offers a quiet environment for studying, with many cultural activities nearby. For those looking for a solid education and a friendly atmosphere, I highly recommend this university.

name: Hasselt University
text: It was a one year master’s program in which we studied about the reuse of abandoned buildings. How you can reuse existing building stock for new functions with necessary interventions keeping in mind the heritage and architectural values of the building. We did multiple site visits and workshops within Europe including Rome, Wuppertal, Brussels, Maastricht etc. In the second semester, we were allowed to choose our own project for the thesis and design studio.

name: KU Leuven
text: Very good support along the trajectory, exams well spaced out, great and a lot of facilities. And also close to my home! in Leuven, faculties spread across the entire city centre, it is a true student experience. Everyone close to one another, makes it something different.

name: KU Leuven
text: The staff and organization of KU Leuven is quite good. Course schedule are quite intensive for one year master, the time flies really fast when you realize it. But it is good for learning knowledge and skill, you can learn a lot from classes and peers.

name: KU Leuven
text: The academic side at KU Leuven was absolutely amazing but there was too little time to do anything else. I studied Electronics engineering and the couse-works was very extensive. There was mot much social activities inside the university but there were a few by Erasmus committee. Overall, It was a great experience.

name: KU Leuven
text: Studying for a bachelor’s degree in European Studies at KU Leuven is not just an intercultural experience, it is genuinely mind-blowing. Right from the start, you have the freedom to choose where to begin your journey, with four universities across four different countries to pick from for your first year. Then, in the second year, the possibilities expand even further, with fourteen options across Europe to follow your chosen major. Finally, for your last year, you can choose again from four universities to complete your degree.

name: KU Leuven
text: This is a very wellrounded university. They offer a ton of opportunities to help guide students the best they can. Campus life is active. The canteen had decent food and it was easy to connect with other students.

name: University of Antwerp
text: The University of Antwerp is a unique and rare institution that truly values and fosters a global community of students and academics. As an international student, I have been enriched by the experience of studying and living in a new environment, with a new standard of education. Every day has brought new lessons and opportunities for growth. I couldn’t have asked for a better experience.

name: University of Antwerp
text: My experience at the University of Antwerp has been outstanding, thanks to the knowledgeable and supportive staff and lecturers. The university has many support structures in place to help students learn both on-campus and online, and the social scene is vibrant and welcoming. Even though I don’t live on-campus, I’ve found it easy to make friends and get involved in the campus community.

name: University of Antwerp
text: The University of Antwerp provides an amazing environment for students, with so many resources available to them. The university is committed to equality and diversity, and the staff goes out of their way to make sure that students feel comfortable and supported. This is a truly inclusive and welcoming place to study, and I’ve found that it’s helped me to discover who I really am. I can’t imagine a better place to be a student.”

name: University of Antwerp
text: University of Antwerp is one of the leading University of Belgium. It has a quite large number of international students. The quality of education is upto the mark. You learn and implement knowledge practically.

name: University of Antwerp
text: University of Antwerp is one of the top universities in the world and I could see that practically it is well known in quality of education, I like the classes, the competence of the professors and the campus life as well. I would recommend for these who are looking for high quality education to further their studies and carrer.

name: University of Antwerp
text: The school offer a wide teaching curriculum the encompasses a large academic area which will give the students the best information about every concept the they are being thought, making them to have educational experience in their preferred field of study.

name: University of Antwerp
text: My time at the University was truly remarkable; the support I received exceeded all expectations, and I thoroughly enjoyed my educational journey studying at University of Antwerp. The opportunity was nothing short of life-changing, especially considering my initial doubts about attending college. Furthermore, the lecturers were outstanding, providing invaluable assistance and guidance.

name: University of Antwerp
text: What stands out about the University of Antwerp is its scenic campus, the vibrant city atmosphere, and the strong sense of community. The university boasts top-notch faculty and a well-structured curriculum. With numerous opportunities for involvement in university activities, students can form connections and broaden their social circles. Overall, the University of Antwerp offers a welcoming and inclusive environment, guaranteeing students a rewarding educational journey.

name: University of Antwerp
text: University of Antwerp embraces multiculturalism and diversity, nurturing a vibrant community of students from diverse backgrounds. The hands-on experience acquired is instrumental in equipping students with the requisite skills to overcome future career challenges. The study abroad component exposes students to diverse cultures and traditions, enriching their global perspective and cultural awareness. This exposure is particularly advantageous for those aspiring to pursue hospitality careers on an international scale.

name: University of Antwerp
text: I really enjoy the area, resources, and opprtunities. This school does an excellent job at wrangling people from all over the world, so we all have a chance to learn from one another. Similarly, because of the largeness of the institution, there are a lot of resources and equipment to learn and experiment with. The professors, for the most part, all stand on wonderful education and offer me a lot in terms of epistemology and growth.

name: UCLouvain
text: Living in a city made by and for the university. Everything is easy of access and understanding, you’ll quickly find a sense of community and shared experience. there is a large choice of fields, classes and activities.

name: UCLouvain
text: I graduated from UCLouvain (ESPO Faculty) in 2014 with a Master’s degree in Economics, and it was a really formative experience for me. What stood out most was how intellectually challenging the program was. It pushed me to think critically, work independently, and develop strong analytical and writing skills. The interdisciplinary environment was also a big plus—you’re constantly exposed to different perspectives, which makes the learning experience richer. I appreciated the opportunity to take a course in Namur University as well as the Louvain School of Management during my program.

name: UCLouvain
text: Most of my classes were in small groups, usually between 20 and 40 students, which made it easier to participate and stay focused. Only two classes had more than 50 students, so overall, it felt quite personal.

name: UCLouvain
text: Really interesting, plus lots of opportunities, like an Erasmus, an internship (which you can also do abroad) so it’s really relative to real life. I also did a baccalaureate in management sciences and it was great.

name: UCLouvain
text: Université Catholique de Louvain academic excellence is evident in its well-rounded core curriculum and diverse course offerings, which have profoundly impacted my worldview. The professors are experts in their fields and dedicated to student success, providing guidance and support when needed.

name: Ghent University
text: Great university, focusing on self discovery. The learning platform they use is very user-friendly. Most professors are very considerate and open to questions. A lot of them are very enthusiastic on their subject as well.

name: Ghent University
text: The Pharmacy program at Ghent University offered a well-balanced mix of theory and practical experience that truly prepared me for a professional career. The curriculum was thoughtfully organized, allowing me to build a deep understanding of pharmaceutical science while developing essential clinical and research skills.

name: Ghent University
text: At Ghent University, I felt truly guided by the psychology faculty, we have experts who are not only passionate researchers but also approachable mentors who make learning personal and inspiring. I had an amazing experience!

name: Ghent University
text: As a PhD student, being part of the department of Biochemistry and Microbiology of UGent I am very pleased with my experience so far. This programme is placed in a setting with leading scientists of the field that lead innovative projects that push the boundaries, providing continuous support by having access to knowledge and expertise of different fields. Also, great care is taken for research integrity, good lab practices and data management plans to ensure the safety of everyone’s work results and their authenticity. It is an inspiring and motivating environment for young researchers with plenty of training opportunities to improve both their hard and soft skills and at the same time creating a strong network. I would definitely recommend it to others.

name: Ghent University
text: The Pharmacy programme provided a strong foundation in both theoretical knowledge and practical training. I particularly appreciated the well-structured curriculum, the opportunity to learn from experienced professors, and the focus on clinical and research skills. The laboratory and internship experiences were very valuable in preparing me for real-world practice. What I liked the most was the diversity of subjects, which gave me a broad understanding of the pharmaceutical field. The international opportunities and access to research projects also added great value to the programme.

name: Université libre de Bruxelles
text: I really enjoyed my time at ULB as I was offered an international program where I could study in English with other international students with no problem. French classes were also provided for free, which was very nice, as well as the gym courses for very little money, which was nice to get to know more people around the campus. I only wish a bit more integration with French-speaking students was supported as I have not met lots of locals, which would have been nice to understand a bit better the local student life.

name: Université libre de Bruxelles
text: The Université Libre de Bruxelles (ULB) is one of those places that leaves a mark on you—not just academically, but culturally and personally. The atmosphere is open, diverse, and driven by a strong spirit of free inquiry. You feel it in the classrooms, in debates, and even in casual conversations around campus.

name: Université libre de Bruxelles
text: ULB has strong academic content and professors who are passionate about their fields. The diversity of students and the international environment are definitely positive aspects, and Brussels as a city gives many opportunities. However, the organisation is often confusing, administrative support can be very slow, and communication with students is sometimes unclear. If you are independent and proactive, you can make the most of it, but if you expect close guidance, it can be frustrating. Overall, I would recommend ULB to motivated students who want solid academic training and don’t mind navigating a bit of chaos along the way.

name: Université libre de Bruxelles
text: This university really has given a great importance to include people from all over the world , different ethnicities backgrounds and cultures. The campus is big and nice and the library is spacious and a great place to study and relax . The common areas are clean and quite comfortable As for the master itself , it has really gone beyond my expectations. Although I has a problem with the language , most professors and indulgent and respect students whose first language is not French ,

name: Université libre de Bruxelles
text: Overall, I would rate my programme at ULB very positively. It is a lively and engaging environment, with the advantages of a human-sized university. The campus is enriched by many exchange students, which creates a strong international atmosphere. In addition, ULB offers plenty of opportunities — both academic and extracurricular — that allow students to grow personally and professionally

name: University of Liège
text: The University of Liege has been a whirlwind of new experiences, both culturally and academically. The academic standards are high, with a more rigorous approach compared to other schools. However, the quality of education and research opportunities are exceptional. Beyond academics, there’s a vibrant social scene on campus with plenty of activities to enjoy. The University provides a supportive environment where everyone can thrive and connect with like-minded individuals.

name: University of Liège
text: The school offers student with a wide range of choices of course to study with adequate facilities to backup each course choice. The school has facilities that will enable the students to excel in their preferred fields.

name: University of Liège
text: Being a student at University of Liege has been an amazing experience. The faculty and staff provide incredible support, consistently going above and beyond to help students succeed. The campus’s sense of history and tradition contributes to a welcoming and comfortable atmosphere.

name: University of Liège
text: Known for its systematic instructional approach and tranquil study atmosphere, University of Liege excels. The institution provides an impressive variety of courses and well-equipped facilities, catering to every student’s needs. I strongly encourage prospective students to consider this university for their higher education.

name: University of Liège
text: University of Liege is an extraordinary institution that excels at both education and research. Its world-class instructors, collaborative environment, and cutting-edge technical resources help students succeed and innovate. The institution provides a variety of courses for students to choose from, with professors focused on providing a thorough comprehension of the course material.

name: Vrije Universiteit Brussel
text: The VUB students are very chill and very open minded. One thing that stands out compared to other universities, is the expertise of the teachers at the VUB. They are true experts in their disciplines.

name: Vrije Universiteit Brussel
text: After graduating from a Master’s in Digital Communication at this University, I have accomplished academic writing skills, group work and learned more about the EU public policies. The only thing that I missed from this master’s programme was integration into the job market and more practical skills in job hunting.

name: Vrije Universiteit Brussel
text: I am really enjoying studying at the VUB! It is a very open-minded and international university where everyone can truly be themselves. The professors are approachable, and the assistants are even more: we can often even call them by their first name! I like the freedom you get to choose your own courses according to your interests, especially in Biology, where you can design your master’s programme almost entirely based on courses you really love. The campus also really feels like a real campus which I love. What I liked a bit less is the administration at the VUB, which can sometimes be confusing or difficult to understand. But I’ve also heard the same from friends at other universities, so I think that’s quite common everywhere. Overall, I would really recommend the VUB.

name: Vrije Universiteit Brussel
text: The facilities of the university are excellent, the campus is really green and beautiful. Professors are mostly supportive and competent. When it comes to the study programme it was closely linked to practice. Overall, what I liked most in my experience of studying here is there are many international students from different nationalities, so it’s quite diverse.

name: Vrije Universiteit Brussel
text: I really like the lush and green atomosphere surrounding the campus on Etterbeek. There is generally always a vivid and positive vibe on the campus. From all the campuses I have been, this one has the most greenery. A small downside however is that some aula’s look a bit dated from the inside, yet this isn’t much of an issue when it comes to my learning experiences.

name: University of Basel
text: The University of Basel has a large international student population and promotes a multicultural environment. This diversity enriches classroom discussions and social interactions. I particularly liked the hosting of various events, including lectures, workshops and cultural festivals which was done through Student Clubs and Organizations, because these activities allowed students to engage with the community and broaden their horizons. I would definitely recommend the University of Basel to anyone interested in studying in a multicultural environment.

name: University of Basel
text: The university’s research opportunities are unparalleled, with state-of-the-art facilities and renowned faculty. The curriculum is regularly updated and needs, ensuring graduates are well-prepared for successful careers. The campus community is inclusive and welcoming, with numerous clubs and organizations. The teachers are friendly and accommodating.

name: University of Basel
text: This university offers diverse academic programs and modern facilities that support a conducive learning environment. Faculty members are knowledgeable and accessible, enhancing the academic experience. The campus is well-maintained, providing a pleasant atmosphere for both study and social activities. Teachers are well trained professionals that guides the students in all academic activities and otherwise.

name: University of Basel
text: The Business and Economics Bachelor at the University of Basel is a very complete and interesting program that gives you all the knowledge you will need to either start a Master’s degree or to start working. There are a lot of classes you can choose from and with that, you have the possibility to give more emphasis to the subjects you are more interested in. The teachers are also very supportive. The only negative point I would highlight is the fact that the university is not the highest-ranking university in Business and Economics of Switzerland, which can make your degree a little less effective to enter a renowned university for your Master’s degree.

name: University of Basel
text: Studying at the oldest University in Switzerland is a very mixed experience. So let’s begin with the positive side.

name: University of Bern
text: I would definitely recommend the University of Bern to all students who are considering attending it. It has a beautiful campus located right in the city center of one of the most student-friendly cities in Switzerland. The faculty is knowledgeable and the courses are good. I studied Biomedical and Medical Engineering at Master’s level, and the quality of the education is among the best in Europe. The university is also very international, with lectures in English, which facilitates the inclusion and participation of foreign students. German is also widely spoken at the university and the city of Bern, making it appealing and interesting to experience a different language and culture. The cost of living in Bern is, however, high, so I would not necessarily recommend attending the university in case you are self-funding your studies.

name: University of Geneva
text: My 3 years experience at Geneva University was amazing! The MAS in Public Health is very well organised and brings professionels of diferent areas not only from Geneva. The environment is very multicultural and the classes have colleagues from diferent backgrounds/ professions/ countries. Great experience. I recommend it

name: University of Geneva
text: My adventure at UNIGE has begun in 2019, with a surprising beginning of academic career in chemistry. Having discovered I wasn’t set for this field, the University allowed me, as it does to all students, to switch paths smoothly to begin a Bachelor’s degree in psychology. Since then, I have completed it and begun a Master’s degree. University of Geneva is a great public university that is usually accommodating, but lacks engagement in student needs and remains quite superficial. The buildings are great, though some are getting a bit old, but there is no campus to properly speaking, which makes up for a quite deceiving student experience. The study plan in my field is comprehensive, but it’s too much academically oriented, even though the majority of students plan to work in the field. Finally, it would have been great to have a major-minor system, in order to be able to broaden our horizons to other fields.

name: University of Geneva
text: Teachers and professors are qualified and professional. The university is one of the best universities in the world and it has an excellent reputation. Many students from different backgrounds and cultures come every year to study in this university and to get a diploma with quality.

name: University of Geneva
text: In general, I liked the management subject as supply chain management. That is why I am now doing a master’s in supply chain management at the business school of Vienna. I think that a bachelor’s is not enough to find a proper job.

name: University of Geneva
text: It is true that a diploma from a Swiss university isn’t easy to get, but it is totally profitable. First, this program gives us opportunities to meet prestigious Swiss businessmen and businesswomen. Plus our professors are well-known and are the best in their domain. With a diploma from UNIGE, we have a career assured, especially if we want to work in Switzerland (one of the countries with the highest wages), but also internationally. Our university is located in the international city of Geneva, we can enjoy the little but pleasant student life with students from all around the world.

name: University of Lausanne
text: My university offers a solid academic environment with knowledgeable lecturers who are approachable and supportive. Courses are well structured and practical, helping students build real-world skills. The facilities are modern, and campus life encourages collaboration, confidence, and personal growth, making the overall experience worthwhile.

name: University of Lausanne
text: Once you enter, there is very little information for the new students so having a friend in another grade is still better than reading all the requirements in French (if you’re an international student). In the master of management – Marketing orientation, there are a lot of people and good friends to make. There are also a lot of student events organised by students for students. As for the program, there’s a lot of groupwork so chose wisely your group member! The best is their alumni program and the network fair which helps when trying to expand your network.

name: University of Lausanne
text: As a student at the University of Lausanne in Arts (French-History), I have often been very critical of my university. I think that the faculty of letters is a complicated faculty that implies the effort of the student, because we are literally part of the courses, since the system is based on the method of seminars given by the students. This system has its strengths and weaknesses, like any system. On the one hand, it’s about listening to our rarely charismatic classmates give the lecture, but on the other hand, when it’s your turn, it’s about immersing yourself in the subject and getting involved in the professor’s lecture and educational line.

name: University of Lausanne
text: I have been studying at the University of Lausanne since 2017. I changed my major in 2018 which was very easy and probably the best decision ever. I have been studying English and History since then and excepting my bachelor degree in June of 2022. The teachers I have encountered are very dedicated to the success and improvement of their students. They were always behind me, helping me and encouraging me to pursue my studies. They gave me useful tools to improve in the many subjects that are available at the university. They try to respond to your emails very quickly and their answers usually surpass the simple question I was asking. Overall, I have had a great experience out there!

name: University of Lausanne
text: University of Lausanne is pretty cool. We can make a lot of sports for free. There is the lake with an amazing view. In summer, we can make barbecue and go swimming after courses. I’m studying in Geopolis, a nice building, with a cool bar, Zelig (it could be bigger). I’m having good times in Lausanne =)

name: Università della Svizzera italiana
text: A great university reality in the heart of Switzerland! Although small, it has a great potential, bringing you very close to the academic world and to an international perspective. The faculty and the students make up a very diverse and stimulating community. It is definitely a hub for Switzerland and a launchpad for an international career. Top scholars and researchers are connected to this university. The style of classes is very suitable for an international curriculum and courses are taught in English. Campus life is accessible, everything is reachable with public transportation with the guarantee of Swiss quality! I have completed my university studies here, and I couldn’t have made a better decision.

name: University of Zurich
text: The students and the whole university populace is so welcoming even if you are not like them. I come from a foreign country , I am an international student, but not even once I felt side lined or discriminated.

name: University of Zurich
text: Facilities here are designed to make studying effective and enjoyable. The library is large, well stocked, and always accessible, while the computer labs and workshops provide everything needed for research and practice. Education is structured to prepare students for future challenges, blending knowledge with useful skills. Teachers are approachable, enthusiastic, and deeply committed to helping every student succeed. The campus is safe, tidy, and filled with inspiring spaces for both learning and relaxation. All these elements combine to create an academic experience that feels professional, balanced, and rewarding.

name: University of Zurich
text: This institution places a high importance on education through planning and commitment. Courses move at a steady pace, giving students time to process each concept before going on. Teachers present lessons with care and professionalism. Classrooms and labs are kept clean and in working order, which makes studying comfortable on a daily basis. The campus itself is still peaceful and well-designed, providing students with a distraction-free learning environment. It’s an environment where careful preparation and unambiguous guidance foster genuine confidence.

name: University of Zurich
text: This university promotes steady learning through well-planned courses and considerate instruction. To help students feel ready for more complex subjects, lessons begin with the fundamentals and gradually progress. Teachers ensure clarity in each class by bringing patience and dedication. Every study session is made easier by the dependable facilities, which include clean labs and well-organized classrooms. With its quiet walkways and serene study areas, the campus layout aids students in staying on course. As a result, students can concentrate entirely on their academic journey in this setting.

name: University of Zurich
text: This university takes great care to ensure that its educational programs are clear. Step-by-step lesson planning ensures that students grasp the subject matter. When help is needed, teachers are approachable and dedicated to upholding high teaching standards. The facilities are tidy, useful, and easily accessible, particularly the labs and classrooms. The campus itself provides students with the quiet, orderly environment they require to learn in peace and quiet throughout the day. It is the perfect setting for steady academic growth.

name: Ludwig Maximilian University Munich
text: Studying History at LMU let’s you choose between a lot of different areas of study, from different epochs and regions. The various libraries and archives/institutions offer a opportunities to research in depth and build up a network for later career steps. The only thing that could be better is to have more books and articles accessible online, subscriptions only cover a part of the content of large databases like Jstor.

name: Ludwig Maximilian University Munich
text: the education system was extensive and the staff was cooperative, but i wish the course allocation system was more student-friendly. there is research opportunities and international exposure, but note that some courses had tuition fees.

name: Ludwig Maximilian University Munich
text: The education system is quite extensive, and the staff is very cooperative. – There’s the option of finishing studies within 5 semesters, which was extended to 6 semesters due to Corona. – The halls where classes are conducted are well-equipped.

name: Ludwig Maximilian University Munich
text: LMU Munich provides a solid academic foundation for BBA students with high quality teaching and a strong research oriented method. All the professors are highly talented and qualified. Studying here also offers great exposure to a major business hub. International environment in uni helps the students to ve more proactive and motivate learning more. This programme is demanding and requires self discipline but it is a highly reputable uni that I would recommend to academically motivated students.

name: Ludwig Maximilian University Munich
text: Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich (LMU) is one of Germany’s most prestigious universities, blending centuries of tradition with modern academic excellence. Students often describe their time there as intellectually stimulating, culturally rich, and personally challenging.

name: RWTH Aachen University
text: Somewhat high turnover rate for staff in my degrees (e.g. History) and apparent focus on technical fields rather than social studies, but helpful for my career, and Aachen is a great place overall with short distances and good public transport

name: RWTH Aachen University
text: Overall the experience has been good, level of materials and teaching depends on professors and institute, however the overall level is good. The theoretical things can be hard and stressful, but you can get through if you invest the time and energy. The reputation of the University in the industry is also great and helpful for finding a job.

name: RWTH Aachen University
text: There are a lot of interesting course that can be elected, the exams are all doable, although some are easier than others. The food in the cafeteria is very good. There is a nice sports program, with again many courses.

name: RWTH Aachen University
text: RWTH is one of the top universities in Germany without a doubt. When it comes to high-tech classes, it is undoubtedly one of the best universities. But, everything has its “dark side” or negative part, and so does RWTH. Living in Aachen was one of the hardest parts of my life, from never being able to find decent accommodation to the weirdest public transport, my life was so chaotic during that time. I literally had to travel around 30 minutes by bus, just to go from one class to another, this was one of the hardest parts of living and studying there. Moreover, one of the most irritating things that caused me a lot of trouble was finding a place to study, I always had to either go from one place to another just to find out that I could not find a place to study, so, I ended up wasting my whole day without studying, or I had to spend extra money to go to some cafes to find some quiet places to study (Espresso Perfetto was the best so far). Of course, I know that there should not be a lot of complaints, but unfortunately, I never had this intro from anyone, and I think that, new students have to know what are they going to deal with once they decide to study at RWTH, and most importantly live in Aachen.

name: RWTH Aachen University
text: I am very pleased with the education material the RWTH provides. It is an university focussed in creating highly informed and experts in the sectors of their studies. Although the types of teaching and quality of teaching is overall quite bad. Compared to other unis there are not many assignments or visualizations of the things presented in the lectures. No chance to gain practical knowledge is given and also developement of personal skills and softskills in the education are severly missing. Participating in student associations to gain the latter mentioned skills are not well supported by the university and is therefore not encouraged at all (for example giving credit points). Yes, at the end of your studies you are an expert on your field but lack personal skills.

name: Technical University of Munich
text: Studying at the Technical University of Munich (TUM) has been a very positive experience, especially because of the flexible academic system. The course enrollment is highly versatile, meaning you can register for almost any subject that interests you. For students who genuinely love to learn, TUM offers a great environment that is both student-friendly and education-focused. Furthermore, the professors and fellow students are very approachable and always willing to support you. Outside of regular classes, there are many opportunities to develop your skills. The university organizes numerous cultural, educational, and career events throughout the year. There are also plenty of research and practical projects to join. If you are interested in business, TUM is exceptionally supportive of startups, especially through initiatives like the TUM Venture Labs, which help students turn their ideas into real companies. However, there is one important disadvantage for international students to consider. TUM recently introduced tuition fees for non-EU/EEA students, which can range from €2,000 to €6,000 per semester. This makes studying here a bit expensive for those from outside Europe. Despite this financial obstacle, the high quality of education and the rewarding environment make TUM a fantastic choice for your future.

name: Technical University of Munich
text: TUM offers excellent engineering and research programs, modern campuses, and strong industry connections. Munich is exciting but costly. Competitive, international, and career-focused. Top choice.!! really good

name: Technical University of Munich
text: The TUM has a lot of money, which is beneficial to organise a lot of different courses.

name: Technical University of Munich
text: Overall great university. Most of the lectures are on your own time, so studying is mostly self-paced. Sometimes because of that it can be challenging, especially depending on the tutors. But nice you can really make a school/life balance. Just during the exam periods is really stressful, especially in electrical engineering because all the exams are very close together.

name: Technical University of Munich
text: I had a very negative experience. Although the student permit permits work, reporting employment to the school can result in a lower GPA. Additionally, many courses are scheduled to overlap, which prevents students from taking two in-demand classes simultaneously. Your grades were fine until you went to the study room—even when you used social media. But if you stay home and study hard, you will get a bad grade.

name: Aalborg University
text: Aalborg University offers an extraordinary learning experience, thanks to its dedicated teachers and well-designed courses. The teachers are knowledgeable and enthusiastic, resulting in an engaging academic environment that promotes student participation and practical application of knowledge. I’ve had the opportunity to learn from knowledgeable instructors who are always willing to help, and I’ve formed great connections with my classmates. The university’s diverse population and innovative approach to education have made my stay here absolutely unforgettable.

name: Aalborg University
text: For me, Aalborg Universitet is a place where academic excellence meets a genuine sense of community. What initially drew me to AAU was its unique approach to problem-based learning, where we tackle real-world challenges rather than just studying theoretical concepts. But what keeps me here is the sense of belonging. The university is incredibly student-centered, and there’s a true culture of collaboration. Professors know you by name, and there’s always an open-door policy for discussing ideas or challenges. AAU places a strong emphasis on teamwork, preparing students to work effectively in diverse, international teams. This has been invaluable in preparing me for my future career. The university’s location in Aalborg is also a major plus; it’s a vibrant city with a mix of modernity and tradition, offering a great quality of life while being just the right size to not feel overwhelming. It’s a place where both academic and personal growth is nurtured.

name: Aalborg University
text: Aalborg University excels in providing research opportunities and guidance to its students. The faculty’s dedication to cutting-edge research reflects in their teaching methods, offering students a chance to engage with the latest advancements in their fields. The university’s well-established research centers and labs provide an immersive experience for those inclined towards academia. The mentorship I’ve received has been instrumental in shaping my research interests, and the university’s focus on fostering a research-driven culture has been a significant asset to my academic journey.

name: Aalborg University
text: Aalborg University’s commitment to an inclusive learning environment sets it apart. As an international student, I’ve felt welcomed and supported throughout my journey. The faculty’s approachability and the university’s focus on student well-being have made my academic experience here truly enriching. The diverse student body allows for a variety of perspectives in class discussions, contributing to a holistic understanding of the subjects. Aalborg University not only values diversity but actively promotes it, creating a sense of belonging for students from all walks of life.

name: Aalborg University
text: As an international student, Aalborg University has been an enriching experience. The welcoming atmosphere and the university’s commitment to diversity create an environment where students from around the world feel at home. The academic programs are not only comprehensive but also offer a global perspective, preparing us for an interconnected world. The faculty’s approachability and willingness to engage with students foster a sense of community. The campus facilities are top-notch, providing a conducive space for learning and collaboration. Aalborg, with its friendly locals and cultural events, adds an extra layer to the overall student experience. I’m grateful for the opportunities and friendships I’ve gained during my time here.

name: Aarhus University
text: Studying at Aarhus University has been a journey of learning, evolving, and… figuring out how to survive the grading system. I’m in the Erasmus Mundus programme, and honestly, the professors here are absolute legends—they go out of their way to help us and make everything super smooth. Love that!

name: Aarhus University
text: Aarhus University really supports its students with lots of study resources,from textbooks to online tools. They are carefully chosen to fit each student’s unique learning style, making learning more engaging and fun.I Highly Recommend!

name: Aarhus University
text: The institution provides a stimulating atmosphere for learning that promotes both autonomous and group thinking. The library is a library of academic resources, and the labs are completely furnished with the newest technology. Teachers are enthusiastic about what they teach and put a lot of effort into making sure that pupils grasp the important ideas. Studying and having fun are perfectly balanced on this vibrant yet serene campus. This university equips students for success with top-notch resources and a lively campus.

name: Aarhus University
text: I’m thrilled to be part of Aarhus University’s community, which prioritizes academic excellence and supports students every step of the way. Accessible professors and advisors have been instrumental in my success, offering guidance and mentorship that has enhanced my learning experience. I feel confident in achieving my goals, thanks to the university’s commitment to a supportive environment that fosters growth and success.

name: Aarhus University
text: My AARHUS university experience has been remarkable, offering countless opportunities for academic and extracurricular engagement. The vibrant community has made my journey unforgettable, allowing me to build lasting connections with peers and professors. I’m committed to making the most of my time here, embracing every opportunity and creating lifelong memories.

name: Aarhus University
text: I would like to study the bachelor’s degree in Economics and Business Administration at Aarhus University next year. I would like to know whether the study environment is quite competitive and if the bachelor’s is highly demanding too.

name: Aarhus University
text: Could you please tell me more about the accommodation? Are there student halls or do students get a flat/apartment? How much does it cost? Do I have a chance of getting a place in the students’ hall?

name: Copenhagen Business School
text: I’m really impressed by how much the professors care about our progress. They regularly check in to make sure everyone understands the material, which makes a big difference.

name: Copenhagen Business School
text: Copenhagen Business School offers a unique blend of academic rigor and practical experience, making it an excellent choice for aspiring professionals, especially in fields like investment banking and consulting. With strong ties to top-tier companies, the job prospects are promising, bolstered by a robust network of alumni and industry connections. The Danish government provides financial support (SU) for local and EU students, alleviating the financial burden and allowing for a focus on academics and career-building activities. The curriculum is well-structured, offering ample free time for part-time jobs, student clubs, or pursuing personal interests. The balance between studies and extracurriculars nurtures a dynamic student experience, while the international environment fosters a global perspective. CBS equips students with not just a degree but a solid foundation in real-world business practices, making graduates highly competitive in the job market.

name: Copenhagen Business School
text: Copenhagen business school impresses me every single time I think about the school as a whole. What’s particularly impressive is how they always check in to ensure everyone is on the same page. Whether it’s through small group discussions or one-on-one feedback sessions, the professors really take the time to make sure no one is left behind. They’re always available for follow-up questions and give tailored feedback that helps students understand where they can improve and how to apply what they’ve learned to real-world situations. It’s clear that at CBS, the focus is on learning, not just getting paid. The professors are not just there to deliver lectures but are deeply invested in making sure every student is progressing.

name: Copenhagen Business School
text: The professors at Copenhagen Business School are genuinely excellent and play an essential role in developing students with extensive real-world experience. What stuck out to me was how supportive they are outside of lectures and class time. They are always willing to answer inquiries, whether concerning the course material or career advice, and many of them work beyond of usual hours to assist students.The instructors’ accessibility fosters an environment in which you feel encouraged, not only intellectually, but also in terms of personal growth and future career goals. It’s apparent that they care significantly about helping students achieve.

name: Copenhagen Business School
text: I think that the courses in the programme are insightful if you are into business and political science, however, as the programme contains both you will not get as much into depth with the topics as if you’d only had one of them. The school itself is okay, administratively I believe that there have been taken many bad decisions lately that restrict the social lives of students (for instance closing the student bar) and the shutdown of several study programmes of a social science character (making it less attractive for many women to attend the school).

name: Technical University of Denmark
text: DTU is a hotpot of international students that all have the same aim – learn about something they are passionate about. Many courses have interactive coursework projects that help students to learn the content, collaborate with potential future co-workers and interact with teachers to get feedback before submission. Such structure allows for continuous improvement during the course and keeps students in touch with the teachers. In fact, professors thank the students for being present; you will always feel welcome at the lectures. This builds personal relationships with potential future supervisors for special projects, which DTU is highly supportive of. It is easy to contact an academic staff with your own idea and carry out a project of any length for credits. If the idea is business-savvy, you can get funding for further research expenses at DTU SkyLab. Thus, DTU creates a perfect environment for early innovation and development of students’ ideas for real world applications. If you are not into business, then there are many other options for working on projects together with fellow students. There are student organisations, ranging from pottery, to brewing beer (it’s Denmark, duh!) and to fibre arts. If none if these are what you are looking for, DTU is incredibly supportive of creating new student organisations and providing sponsorship for them. So find what you are passionate for and come together with like-minded people to work on a project that will leave your mark on the world!

name: Technical University of Denmark
text: At DTU you get a unique opportunity to dive deep into the world of technology. The university’s advanced facilities and state-of-the-art laboratories provide an ideal setting for hands-on learning and research. The teaching is strongly research-based, and many courses involve projects in collaboration with Danish and international companies. It creates a learning environment that focuses not only on theory but also on practical application of knowledge. The open and creative atmosphere on campus promotes innovation and collaboration across different disciplines. With a focus on sustainability and innovation, DTU is the place for those who want to be at the forefront of future technological development.

name: Technical University of Denmark
text: From the moment I stepped onto the campus, I was impressed by its state-of-the-art research facilities and the collaborative atmosphere that encourages innovation. The university’s strong focus on engineering and technology has provided me with invaluable opportunities to grow both academically and professionally, especially through its hands-on approach and strong industry connections.

name: Technical University of Denmark
text: At the University, I’ve found student interaction to be incredibly enriching, driven by a spirit of collaboration and mutual support. Whether working on group projects or joining student-led organizations, I’ve connected with peers who bring diverse perspectives and unique ideas. The co-op program, in particular, has been a great way to build friendships while sharing real-world experiences, making my academic journey both enjoyable and meaningful.

name: Technical University of Denmark
text: Forget stuffy lectures and textbook-only learning! At DTU, you’re thrown headfirst into real-world challenges. Professors are industry veterans, not just academics, and they bring their practical experience into the classroom. You’ll be building prototypes, collaborating on projects with companies, and even testing your ideas in the field. By the time you graduate, you won’t just have a degree, you’ll have a portfolio of real-world experience that makes the transition from student to professional seamless.

name: IT University of Copenhagen
text: The IT University of Copenhagen (ITU) is an inspiring place to study for anyone passionate about technology. This is the university that truly embraces the future, offering programs in areas like software development, data science, and digital innovation. What makes ITU special is the hands-on approach – students often work on real-world projects alongside teachers and industry professionals, making learning feel practical and engaging. The campus is modern and welcoming, with great facilities that support both study and creativity. Plus, being in the heart of Copenhagen, we are surrounded by a buzzing tech scene, opening up fantastic opportunities for internships and careers. ITU is a place where we can thrive, grow, and be ready to take on the fast-changing world of technology!

name: University of Copenhagen
text: The standard of education is exceptionally high with programs structured to build knowledge progressively across every level of study. Academic content goes beyond textbooks with a strong emphasis on practical application and critical thinking. Teaching staff are highly knowledgeable experienced and consistent in delivering lectures that are both engaging and intellectually demanding. Feedback on assessments is detailed and genuinely useful for improvement. The campus is visually impressive clean and well maintained throughout the year. Facilities including research labs digital suites and the central library are modern regularly upgraded and accessible to all students without unnecessary restrictions.

name: University of Copenhagen
text: The standard of education here is consistently high and you feel that from the very first lecture. Academic programs are rigorous and clearly designed with both knowledge and career readiness in mind. Teachers are exceptional not only in their subject knowledge but in their ability to communicate complex ideas in ways that actually stick. The campus is clean spacious and beautifully designed with outdoor spaces that make it a genuinely pleasant place to spend your days. Computer labs and science facilities are regularly upgraded and the university clearly invests in keeping everything at a high standard.

name: University of Copenhagen
text: The university provides a strong academic foundation through organized programs and effective teaching methods. Courses are planned to help students understand subjects clearly and improve their knowledge over time. Teachers explain lessons in a clear and structured way, helping students follow each topic. They also provide support during assignments and academic discussions. The campus is designed to support learning, with quiet study areas and well-maintained buildings. Facilities such as laboratories and lecture halls support both practical and theoretical learning. The library offers resources that help students succeed in their studies.

name: University of Copenhagen
text: A well-planned academic system supports learning across all programs in the university. Education is delivered through clearly organized courses that guide students from basic understanding to more advanced knowledge. Each subject is arranged in a logical way, making it easier for students to follow lessons and complete their studies successfully. Teachers show strong knowledge of their fields and explain topics in a clear and careful manner. They also provide support during assignments, class discussions, and academic tasks. The campus is quiet and properly arranged, creating a suitable environment for study. Facilities such as lecture halls, laboratories, and computer rooms support both theory and practical work. The library offers a wide range of academic materials for reading and research.

name: University of Copenhagen
text: The university offers a well-organized system for education and learning. Academic programs follow clear guidelines that support steady progress throughout the course of study. Teachers are knowledgeable and explain subjects in ways that are easy to understand. They help students develop confidence in their academic work and provide guidance when needed. The campus environment is calm and encourages students to focus on their studies. Facilities across the university include modern classrooms, laboratories, and comfortable study areas. The library also provides useful books and research materials that help students strengthen their academic knowledge.

name: Roskilde University
text: I have a positive attitude towards RUC. I enjoy the project-based learning system. It helped my learning to use real-life problems. I also gained better research, writing and presentation skills. Teachers are supportive and helpful. I enjoy the international atmosphere of the university. Students can exchange their ideas from various countries. The first time I used the system it was a little tricky. Students need to be independent and manage their own time. Sometimes group work can be difficult. However, it gave me the confidence and responsibility. Yes, I would be recommending RUC to other students.

name: Roskilde University
text: Studying MSc in Business Administration and Leadership at Roskilde University has been an excellent experience for me. I truly appreciate the learning environment, which is friendly, open, and highly supportive. The teaching approach is interactive and encourages active participation, while the emphasis on group work makes the learning process both engaging and meaningful. As an international student, I feel very comfortable and well-integrated into the academic community. Overall, I am extremely satisfied with the programme it has been a valuable and enriching journey, and I would highly recommend it to others.

name: Roskilde University
text: The reasons I chose to study here is because Roskilde University encourages interdisciplinary studies, allowing students to explore a wide range of topics and fields. The university is known for its student-centered approach to learning, which includes problem-based and project-based learning. The university has a diverse student body and offers various international programs, contributing to a rich and multicultural learning experience. Roskilde University is recognized for its high-quality academic programs and excellent faculty members. But the university has limited courses, certain specialized fields are not represented. Roskilde University is located outside of Copenhagen, which may be a downside for students who prefer to study in a more urban environment. Non-Danish-speaking students may face challenges adjusting to the language and cultural differences, although the university does offer support for international students.

name: Roskilde University
text: When I started my studies at RUC, my expectations were a mixture of high academic standards with a high degree of competition, what I get at the end of the day was something completely different.

name: University of Southern Denmark
text: If you are looking for a typical engineering university where you just mug up formulas and pass exams, SDU is probably not for you. But if you want to actually build things and understand how future tech works, this place is gold. I am currently studying Electronics here and just finished my 3rd semester. Looking back at my journey so far, the best part is definitely the project-based learning. In many countries, EEE or Electronics is mostly about textbooks and boring lab reports. But at SDU, they push you into the practical world from day one. Even though I am still in the middle of my graduation, I already ended up completing multiple projects, and each one taught me how to solve real life problems. One big thing they focus here is sustainable development. It’s not just a buzzword. The professors actually taught us how to adapting high-tech engineering with green energy, so that whatever we build helps the world in the long run. The coolest project I worked on recently was about magnetic levitation. We literally built a system where a platform could float and move over magnetic tiles on the floor, without any wheels at all! Dealing with that kind of frictionless, sci-fi technology in my early semesters was mind-blowing and honestly, it taught me more about electromagnetism than any textbook ever could. Overall, SDU doesn’t just make you an engineer, it changes how you look at technology. Highly recommended if you want hands-on experience.

name: University of Southern Denmark
text: I’m currently pursuing my M.Sc. in Electronic Engineering at SDU’s Sønderborg campus, and it has genuinely shaped the way I think as an engineer. The programme is two years long, and every semester feels purposefully designed. SDU doesn’t just teach you engineering it teaches you how to think at the frontier of technology. The university has a strong focus on green energy, which isn’t just a marketing label here. It’s embedded into how courses are structured and what research directions are encouraged. One subject that has truly stood out for me is Embedded Power Systems. What I love most about it is how it bridges theory directly to real green energy applications, you’re not solving abstract problems, you’re working on systems that actually matter for the energy transition. It’s the kind of course that makes you realise why you chose engineering in the first place. The Sønderborg campus is small and quiet a very different from a big university city. As an international student, that takes some adjustment. But the focused, calm environment actually helps you concentrate on your studies, and the student community is close-knit because of it. The hands-on teaching culture means you’re constantly building, experimenting, and developing your own ideas alongside new technologies. For future students considering SDU Sønderborg, come prepared to think independently, embrace a quieter lifestyle, and dive deep into cutting-edge engineering. It’s absolutely worth it.

name: University of Southern Denmark
text: I spent a semester as an exchange student in the College of Humanities at SDU, and it was an incredibly enriching experience. Coming from Taiwan, I found the academic environment in Denmark to be very collaborative and open.

name: University of Southern Denmark
text: My experience at the University of Southern Denmark (SDU) was both academically enriching and personally transformative. SDU offers a strong learning environment that combines theoretical depth with practical application, encouraging students to think critically and independently. The teaching style was interactive and student-centered, with lecturers who were approachable, knowledgeable, and supportive. I particularly valued the emphasis on problem-solving, group work, and real-world case studies, which helped me develop skills that are highly relevant beyond the classroom.

name: University of Southern Denmark
text: good environment with friendly professors and colleagues. perfect for gaining more knowledge specific to the field of study and well as focused on overall sustainability improvement. but need more career guidance for both freshers and newcomers./

name: Universidad Autónoma de Barcelona
text: It’s a good univertisy with good reputation. I studied Medicine with good results. After 4 years you hace to choose a universitary hospital to finish the last two years. You must know that it is en Catalonia, and some classes are done in catalan language!

name: Universidad Autónoma de Barcelona
text: The UAB is honestly a great place to study. The campus is huge and super lively, with tons of green areas and stuff going on all the time. Most professors really know their field and are pretty approachable, which makes classes way easier to handle. Plus, the campus has everything you need libraries, food spots and transport, so daily life flows pretty smoothly. Overall, good vibes, solid academics, and a campus that feels like its own little city.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Barcelona
text: Located right next to lush green forest, this is a wonderful university with great faculties, excellent sporting facilities and good options for food and dorms. It is self sufficient and also has a train station attached to it, offering extremely good connectivity with the neighbouring towns.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Barcelona
text: While doing my PhD, I feel very supported not only by my PhD advisors but for other professors of the Math department too. Moreover, the environment of the university is really good and the digital resources (books, articles, etc) are a lot and really useful.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Barcelona
text: Good University but 4 years of studying should prepare the students better to work in the psychology field. There is no personal work or experience of therapy, it’s focused on theory and not on real learning. The neuroscience classes in general were very good, and some teachers are amazing. But in general I would have expected better quality from a recognised University.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Madrid
text: Pretty nice people and professors. There are many different options and specializations to choose your preferred path in science. Mny opportunities for young researchers to develop their skills in a professional environment.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Madrid
text: Good quality of research. Opportunities for undergrad students to learn and develop reseach skills in a professional environment. Good programs, with many different specializations to choose your preferred path in science.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Madrid
text: Probably one of the best universities in Spain. The academic standards are high, but the faculty is up to the task. Graduates typically have good career prospects. The facilities are excellent if you want to combine your studies with sports and a wide range of extracurricular activities.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Madrid
text: The course is very complete, the structure could be improved by being more organized but overall I’ve learned a lot and I like the teachers. Campus life could be improved by a few little things, I would like to see more diversity.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Madrid
text: It’s a university where the professors strive to enhance your personal and professional skills, both generally and individually, so you can reach your full potential. It helped me hone my interpersonal skills, enabling me to become the successful and ambitious professional I am today.

name: Universidad de Barcelona
text: My experience at the University of Barcelona has its pros and cons. Academically and in terms of prestige, it’s good, and there are some truly worthwhile professors, although some courses have become completely outdated. However, the internal management, bureaucracy, and communication with students leave much to be desired. You’re very dependent on the luck of the draw with your professors, because the faculty’s organization isn’t always helpful, and some facilities are outdated. It’s a good university, it serves its purpose, but it has many things that need improvement on a daily basis.

name: Universidad de Barcelona
text: Honestly, I didn’t attend class that often, so my connection to the university was more practical than emotional. I appreciated the resources, but I felt disconnected from campus culture. In hindsight, I’d have engaged more, but I also believe that university should facilitate more digital resources and support for those who, for whatever reason, can’t always attend in person.

name: Universidad de Barcelona
text: Great university with a friendly vibe and lots of international students. The campus is beautiful, the professors are knowledgeable, and studying in Barcelona makes the whole experience even better. Definitely a great place to be a student.

name: Universidad de Barcelona
text: I spent a semester in Barcelona for my Erasmus programme as part of my Master’s in Work, Organizational, and Personnel Psychology. During that time, I had the opportunity to work on my thesis and attend classes with renowned scholars in the field. Studying abroad and collaborating with international students from diverse cultural backgrounds really enhanced my global perspective and intercultural skills. Barcelona itself is an amazing city, full of colour, culture, and life. It’s the perfect place to combine academic growth with personal development

name: Universidad de Barcelona
text: My experience at Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona was very positive. The professors were knowledgeable, approachable, and supportive, making classes engaging and intellectually stimulating. The campus itself is large, green, and full of resources, including libraries, study spaces, and social areas where it’s easy to meet new people.

name: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid
text: I did my Bachelor (bilingual) and Master in Computer Science at UC3M and my experience was good overall. You find professors that are really passionate and the collorary is pretty broad, while allowing you to go a bit deeper whenever you are interested in something, mainly through the practical projects. The first 2 years are always quite Math intensive but then it gets fun. I think it is better to take it easy and enjoy it a bit instead of pushing through the four years, because if you want to really do all the practice assignments you will find yourself with basically zero free time. Definitely would recommend the bilingual path since also groups are reduced and you can really feel the difference in the teaching

name: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid
text: It was a really good place, as it,s a public funded University the expectations of investment could be considered low but it isn,t. Here are great teachers with profesional and PhD backgrounds, good instalations and incredible bookstore.

name: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid
text: The school has an excellent curriculum. The curriculum is designed to make the process of teaching and learning for both teachers and students very easy. Also the school has top notch facilities for students to study and learn, it also makes learning process easier

name: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid
text: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid (UC3M) stands out as one of Spain’s top universities for engineering, offering a well-balanced mix of theoretical knowledge and practical experience. The Leganés campus is modern, well-equipped, and designed to foster innovation with state-of-the-art labs and strong ties to industry through its Science and Technology Park.

name: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid
text: The Universidad Carlos III de Madrid prioritizes recruiting the most exceptional and proficient professors to provide students with the best possible education. Furthermore, the institution’s dedication to students’ mental well-being is evident through the employment of skilled counselors who offer a safe space for students to discuss their mental health issues and make informed choices about their lives.

name: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid
text: Hi, I don’t know if this topic is sensitive to ask but as someone who is interested in studying Law and business administration at UC3M I really want to know how much the tuition so that I know if studying there is plausible for my current situation

name: Universidad Complutense de Madrid
text: They could take advantage of the tools and instalations they have to teach the students more practical and effective ways to learn and do the future jobs they are gonna do. Not very focus on finding new resources or equipment, but want to keep the old ways to do things

name: Universidad Complutense de Madrid
text: Old building, old technology and poorly organized. Some professors are good but the lessons they teach seam poorly organized. Campus doesn’t provide enough clubs and the feeling of family could be better.

name: Universidad Complutense de Madrid
text: In my experience, Complutense University of Madrid is a university with a very high academic standard and a great atmosphere. The best thing about it is the student life and the diversity of people; the worst thing is some disorganization and the somewhat outdated buildings.

name: Universidad Complutense de Madrid
text: Even if sometimes you see how infrafinanced the degrees are, you can still live the best of your university years there. Proffesors down to earth, best clubs to meet people, everyone is so open to meet people!, great events and spontaneous parties, better environment and bars close to the campus. The worst part is when you graduate and dont have to come back!

name: Universidad Complutense de Madrid
text: My personal experience has been very good; even though I’m only just finishing my second year, I can say that I’ve learned a lot. The level of demand is very high, and the degree is much more challenging than I expected, but thanks to that I’m confident I can become a good professional.

name: Universidad Politécnica de Madrid
text: My experience here has been good and I genuinely enjoyed the program. The best part is definitely the academic level and the relevance of the main subjects. On the downside, the internal organization can get a bit chaotic at times, and a couple of classes fell a bit short. All in all, the pros outweigh the cons and I would recommend this university for specializing in this field.

name: Universidad Politécnica de Madrid
text: The level is very hard comparing to the few years it lasts. It should last longer with more hours per subject and the level has to be lower, not expecting us to solve every problem they come up with for the exams.

name: Universidad Politécnica de Madrid
text: My experience at UPM has been mixed. On the positive side, there are many dedicated and passionate professors who are truly committed to teaching, although there are others who are not as engaged. In terms of technology, the university’s laboratories could definitely be improved. The same applies to the classrooms—many of the projectors are outdated and do not display clearly. Considering that UPM is a technological university, this is an aspect that should be upgraded. Another point is that many classrooms still use chalkboards, which should be modernized as well. On the other hand, there are great classmates, and strong friendships can be built, which makes the experience more enjoyable. Overall, despite some areas that need improvement, the university can still be recommended as a place to study.

name: Universidad Politécnica de Madrid
text: The UPM is the best engineering university in Spain, the programs are very complete and demanding, so you have so spend a ton of time studing which can be a bit exhausted.
