Also, campus life, at least in my campus is amazing, you got lots of people from diferents degrees in the same place so you can meet diferent people.

name: Universidad Politécnica de Madrid
text: This university is thrilled about having all the associations in this university, they say it gives to you all the things that the association can give to you that the association cannot. The ongoing knowledge that an NGO can provide it’s not something that can be achieved with only classes

name: Aalto University
text: I had a great experience at Aalto University. I really liked the independent learning approach, which gave me the freedom to study at my own pace. The facilities are excellent, and having access to a wide range of equipment made learning more practical and engaging. Overall, it’s a great place for students who value flexibility and hands-on experience.

name: Aalto University
text: Aalto University is honestly such an amazing place. The campus is right in the middle of nature, with woods and water all around. The student life here is super active, there are tons of student clubs that host different events all over the week, which provide great opportunities to make some new friends!

name: Aalto University
text: I love that you can find support everywhere about any problems at Aalto. They have a great community here that cares about the people. No matter what problem I’m facing, I always know that I could get the help I needed

name: Aalto University
text: There are many facilities and opportunities for the students who seek for them. There is an incredible flexibility for courses you can take and skills you can learn. However, the courses usually span for too little time to grasp the details of the topic.

name: Aalto University
text: In the three years I spent completing my Design BA at Aalto, the facilities at Aalto are definitely the highlight. However, it goes beyond just the machines tools and workshops we use, but the ways in which we are integrated and introduced to them. Through courses, groupwork, and teaching methods, we get to learn through creating. The courses in my program are also very diverse, which is another reason why we get to use and learn about so many different design tools and techniques, ranging from cnc milling and woodworking, to packaging and 3d printing. Aalto is a great place to be for anyone who thrives in a creative and collaborative environment.

name: Aalto University
text: The best thing it has would be the study/life balance, while still giving quality education. It has really competent academic staff in all the fields I took courses in (computer science, mathematics, and business). There is always a student event happening, and there are countless student associations for any hobby/interest. Not to mention all the student traditions that have been kept alive for tens of years.

name: Aalto University
text: Aalto has been the best University for international students. The quality of education is best among the universities. For interntional students, all the Master’s degree are taught in English and it is very easy to get yourself integrated into the learning environment.

name: Aalto University
text: The academic system at this university is built around innovation and growth. The program challenges students to think deeply and approach learning with creativity. Teachers are dedicated professionals who ensure that lessons remain relevant to modern challenges. Their mentorship helps students gain confidence and develop essential problem-solving skills. The campus provides an ideal balance between study and relaxation, offering quiet zones for focus and open areas for collaboration. Facilities such as laboratories, libraries, and research centers are modern, well-equipped, and accessible.

name: Aalto University
text: I would absolutely recommend Aalto. I think the course offering is very broad and fits my needs. The teaching quality is great and the courses are not just mass lectures.

name: Aalto University
text: As a student at Aalto University, I can attest that it’s a unique institution that fosters innovation, creativity, and collaboration. Here’s my honest review:

name: Aalto University
text: Are there any social groups or communities for Italian students at Aalto University?

name: Aalto University
text: I’m planning to get a Master’s in Marketing at Aalto and already have 8 years of experience in international sales in the Middle East. Would someone with this kind of profile be able to find a good job in this field after graduating?

name: Åbo Akademi University
text: Very transformative and supportive environment. All the professors a d staffs are really helpful and I have got my best moment to celebrate as a student parliament member of the EU Parliament. I enjoyed every moment.

name: Åbo Akademi University
text: I like that it is flexible to study, you are not rushed. And i commend how information are passed, especially when it comes to your study needs, my study advisor and head of department are always happy to help

name: Åbo Akademi University
text: The infraestructure is almost perfect. The university is super new, the library super cool and cosy, perfect for studying, and the cafeteria is quite cheap. Locsted in the centercof Turku, its a little university but really good for an international experience. Plenty of courses and master programmes in English. I really felt welcome there.

name: Åbo Akademi University
text: I did my Master’s in Information and Knowledge Management in Abo Akademi and it was truly great experience. I enjoyed the theoretical base I got and also all the facilities that the campus provided. Marketing courses that I had as my minor, provided the opportunities to work on different cases and collaborate within the team. The library is great too. And there are a lot of exchange and international students there, so it’s a unique chance to meet people from all around the World.

name: Åbo Akademi University
text: I spend my semester abroad at Abo Akademi and studied the double master degree Intercultural Advertising. The buildings of the university are quite modern and there are a variety of well-equipped libraries and sport facilities. I can recommend attending the sports classes. Also, the canteens provide different dishes and fresh salad for an affordable price. Overall, the staff and teachers were very helpful, friendly and easy to approach in all matters. In terms of the classes offered in the field of marketing I think they offered a basic understanding and an intercultural approach via several group work activities. However, relating to my master program I would have wished for a greater focus on in-depth knowledge and more individual work. The winter in Finland can seem quite long, but activities like going to the sauna, doing sports, meeting up with friends and traveling to Lapland or Stockholm can contribute to having a good experience studying abroad.

name: University of Helsinki
text: University of Helsinki is widely regarded as one of the strongest universities in the Nordic region. It has a solid academic reputation, especially in research, and offers a diverse international environment in the heart of Helsinki

name: University of Helsinki
text: It brings the best of both worlds: a very top research and teaching environment and one of the best (if not the) cities of the world to live in. Nature, peace, a public sector that works, the happiest country in the world. If you don’t have problems with the cold weather, it is the best place to be. The researching and teaching is also top notch. For PhDs, English is enough. But if you are looking for BS or Masters, than you need to check.

name: University of Helsinki
text: The Master’s programme in Plant Production Sciences has been a very valuable experience for me. It is strongly research focused, which helps me develop important skills such as critical thinking, scientific writing, and data analysis.I really appreciate how the courses are connected to real world agricultural challenges such as sustainability, crop production, and climate change. The programme also covers manyof the latest scientific topics, including developments in plant science and biotechnology. This makes the learning experience more interesting and helps me understand how current research is shaping the future of agriculture. One of the most important parts of the programme is working on research projects. Through this, I gain practical experience in planning experiments, analyzing data, and presenting scientific results. The supervisors are supportive and encouraged independent thinking, which helps me grow academically and personally. I recommend this programme to students who are interested in plant production science and are motivated to develop their research skills, especially if they are considering a future PhD or research career.

name: University of Helsinki
text: Helsinki University is a diverse and cozy university with high quality teaching and research. The teachers are intelligent and kind and easy to talk to. The programs and subjects are well planned out and the work load for courses are usually decent, not too much or too little. I also loved the student culture and clubs on the campus. Also. If you ever need help with your studies you can always get help and guidance to plan your studies or solve problems.

name: University of Helsinki
text: The university is known for its well-crafted programs and high academic standards. Students receive challenging and useful education here, equipping them with the skills they will need for their future careers. Teachers foster a supportive learning environment in the classroom because they are enthusiastic about what they do and always make time for questions. The campus provides a secure learning environment and is contemporary and immaculately maintained. Students have all they need to succeed thanks to dependable and easily accessible facilities like state-of-the-art labs, digital libraries, and recreation areas.

name: University of Helsinki
text: I’ve been admitted to the new Liberal Arts and Sciences programme at University of Helsinki (2026 intake). Since this is the first cohort, I’d love to hear: – How is the academic support for interdisciplinary students? – Are there strong research opportunities early on? – How does Helsinki support international students in building career paths? Please help😭

name: LUT University
text: Studying Electrical Engineering at LUT University feels modern, practical, and strongly industry-oriented. The program focuses on real engineering problems in power systems, electronics, and energy technology, with lots of project-based learning.

name: LUT University
text: From my perspective, my experience at LUT University has been positive overall. I especially liked the strong focus on project-based and practical coursework, which helped me gain real-world skills. The academic environment is calm and research-oriented. However, I felt that the university is not very supportive when it comes to helping international students find jobs. Overall, I would recommend it to students who are independent and proactive.

name: LUT University
text: Studying at LUT University has been a transformative experience, both academically and personally. As a Master’s student in *Global Communications and Clean Air, Water, and Energy*, I had the opportunity to explore important global topics like sustainability, responsible communication, and behavioral change in depth. The courses were thoughtfully designed, and I especially appreciated how theory was consistently linked to real-world applications.

name: LUT University
text: My experience as a graduate student at LUT University in Finland has been incredibly enriching. The academic environment is both rigorous and supportive, with professors who are genuinely invested in our success. The curriculum is well-structured, allowing me to delve deep into my field while also encouraging interdisciplinary collaboration. The weather can be quite a challenge, especially during the long winters, but the beauty of the snowy landscapes makes it worthwhile. I’ve learned to embrace the cold by participating in winter sports and enjoying cozy gatherings with friends. Tuition fees are reasonable compared to many other European universities, and there are various scholarships available for international students. The campus facilities are modern and well-equipped, fostering a conducive learning atmosphere.

name: University of Oulu
text: Oulu combines a compact city life, strong student culture, easy access to nature and high-quality public services. For study environment and university, I don’t have much to complain about. Students have access to excellent facilities for studying and research. Especially, University of Oulu is strong in ICT, wireless technology, heath tech and education. Many English-taught MSc and BSc options.

name: University of Oulu
text: I’m currently pursuing my Master’s in Computer Science at the University of Oulu, and my experience so far has been very positive.

name: University of Oulu
text: This is one of the best universities in Finland. The friendly environment and modern educational facilities make it a self-learning hub where you can explore yourself. The 24-hour campus access also makes it one of the best.

name: University of Oulu
text: The University of Oulu has a strong research focus and a supportive academic culture. As a PhD researcher at Oulu Business School, while working on my research project, I’ve found the environment intellectually stimulating and encouraging of independent thinking. It’s professional, research-driven, and genuinely supportive of long-term academic growth. Furthermore, the university has plenty of cultural events that you can attend!

name: Tampere University
text: I’ve had an incredible experience studying Biomedical Engineering here. The program strikes a really good balance between rigorous engineering concepts and hands-on medical applications. The curriculum is challenging but highly rewarding, and the professors are outstanding experts in their fields. I’ve had access to great lab equipment and a highly supportive student community. If you are looking for a top-tier engineering program with fantastic campus life, I highly recommend this place.

name: Tampere University
text: Tampere University is one of the beautiful universities I have ever seen. Although I have not seen many universities, compared to the place I come from, it is wonderful. I would wholeheartedly recommend this university. It features a blend of local and international flavours. Teachers here are friendly, and the environment is great for studying. There are numerous facilities for students, teachers, as well as researchers. However, I am a little skeptical about my program as it is new, so no one really knows what we should do after completing our degree. Other than that, I really like my program, what I study, and most importantly, with whom I study. I really enjoy the classes because of my classmates and teachers. Campus life here is effective and enjoyable. Students study a lot, but they also want to lead more fulfilling lives. There are many organisations and people to help in any situation. Ultimately, I would say I am fortunate to have the opportunity to study here.

name: Tampere University
text: Tampere University is a great school to study in a diverse culture and integrate with internationals and also it give you an opportunity to explore other fields of study other than your programme which lets you discover your own interest and maybe build new skills over the period of time. Also, the student culture is amazing.

name: Tampere University
text: When I first arrived in Tampere as an international student from a Muslim country, I was anxious and self-conscious. I often worried about being judged for my clothing or for practicing my faith. However, the open-minded and respectful environment in Tampere quickly changed my perspective. People here are focused on their own lives and give others the space to be themselves. I’ve never felt discriminated against based on my gender or religion.

name: Tampere University
text: As an aspiring student from Bangladesh, I, Md Mehedi Hasan, wanted to advance my education and profession by earning a better degree from Finland. After extensive work and research, I discovered the Science and Engineering programme at Tampere University, Finland. As a new student at Tampere University, I’ve gained valuable insights and experiences that extend beyond academics. The fuksi events offered a warm introduction to university life, fostering connections and immersing me in Finnish traditions. The modern libraries and labs on the Hervanta campus have been helpful in enhancing my academic journey, providing access to resources and hands on learning opportunities. Additionally, the variety of student events and activities has enriched my experience, encouraging personal growth, collaboration, and exploration. Tampere University has already helped me build knowledge, skills, and friendships that are shaping my future.

name: University of Turku
text: UT is a fantastic choice for driven students. The info technology program I’m enrolled in is well-organized, with approachable professors who truly care about our success. Turku is a vibrant city with a rich cultural heritage and a welcoming atmosphere, enhancing the overall experience. The university cultivates a strong sense of community and provides ample opportunities for both academic and personal development.

name: University of Turku
text: Having lived in the Student Village for over a month, and it’s been fine. The apartments are renovated, but if you move into one of the older blocks, you share the kitchen with others. The Village is much closer to campus, saving you on bus fares—unless you choose to bike in every day. Honestly, you can’t really cycle around in –27 °C! But if you give it a go, it’s actually pretty fun.

name: University of Turku
text: An excellent place for motivated students. The health technology program I’m part of is well-structured, and the professors are approachable and genuinely invested in student success. Turku itself is a beautiful city with a rich history and peaceful vibe, which adds to the overall experience. The university fosters a great sense of community and offers plenty of opportunities for both academic and personal growth.

name: University of Turku
text: accommodation, it met all my needs. Other than that, everything was great. If I needed help, I could just hang out downstairs to see my classmates, and we often had student hangouts in the evenings because we lived so close to each other. The proximity of everyone was a big advantage, despite the 20-minute bus ride to school.

name: University of Turku
text: I have also lived in the Student Village for more than a month, that was fine too. The apartments have been renovated, but if you get an apartment in the older part, you share the kitchen with other people. The student village is much closer to the university, saving money on bus tickets – unless I plan to cycle to school every day. However, it must be said that you can’t ride a bike everywhere in -27 degrees! It’s fun if you try.

name: Aix-Marseille Université
text: The teachers were really invested and always easy to talk to and ask questions and explain things we didn’t understand in the first place. We were very lucky to be so well accompanied and not left on our own device right from the start.

name: Aix-Marseille Université
text: It’s a big university but a lot of thing doesn’t work well. There are far too many degrees offered without any career prospects. Furthermore, there is no support available to find an internship or a job after graduation. The school administration is incompetent when they even bother to reply to us.

name: Aix-Marseille Université
text: I really enjoyed my university, especially the neuroscience program it offers. I also completed a Bachelor’s degree in Mathematics there; The overall atmosphere is very positive, and both the teaching staff and the academic programs are of high quality. The university also provides support and opportunities for students to go abroad and find internships, which is a significant advantage. The only downside is that some of the facilities are somewhat outdated. Apart from that, I would definitely recommend this university to people around me.

name: Aix-Marseille Université
text: Everything is not bad, sometimes I wait the results of exams longer than expected . Anyway, all the professors are great, you know, sometimes you just get a good vibe here. They have a really good community to get to know foreign students.

name: Aix-Marseille Université
text: I would highly recommend this university because of its highly acknowledged reputation among European universities. Any kind of bachelor’s or master’s degree would one choose has a high quality training not only in theoretic knowledge but also in practical one. Moreover, the student support is working perfectly, so if someone has any kind of question, they can solve it within minutes. Additionally, this university brings closer the professional world by creating internship forums where students can easily connect with companies in their field.

name: Université de Bordeaux
text: Writing as a Pharmaceutical Sciences student at the University of Bordeaux. I’ve found that this program perfectly combines research, innovation, and practical training. The coursework dives deeply into drug design, formulation, and clinical application, preparing you for real challenges in the pharmaceutical field. The professors are deeply committed to student growth and scientific excellence. With access to good labs and a collaborative environment, the school provides the ideal foundation for anyone passionate about advancing health through science.

name: Université de Bordeaux
text: As a Law student at the Universite de Bordeaux, I’ve gained a good understanding of both French and international legal systems. The program emphasizes critical analysis, ethics, and the evolving role of law in global society. Professors are knowledgeable and approachable, often connecting theory with real legal practice. The university’s cultural diversity and academic depth make every discussion dynamic and enriching. Studying law here isn’t just about memorizing statutes, it’s about learning to think like a global legal mind.

name: Université de Bordeaux
text: Studying at the University of Bordeaux was inspiring—excellent professors, diverse courses, and strong research focus. The city of Bordeaux is vibrant, beautiful, and full of culture, making life enjoyable!

name: Université de Bordeaux
text: The University of Bordeaux is equipped with a conducive learning environment, excellent teaching practices and highly intelligent and sound professors. Class schedule are structured to incorporate practical and theoretical lessons while making room for an improved social life for the students.

name: Université de Bordeaux
text: University of Bordeaux is a great university But I think my program had too many courses for a masters program. In my opinion, a masters program should be focused on a particular domain. But, having alot of courses like innovation, parents, intellectual property rights as different courses was unwarranted. All could have been merged together as one course.

name: École Normale Supérieure (Paris)
text: The École Normale Supérieure (ENS) is one of France’s most prestigious institutions, known for its rigorous education and research-driven approach. With top-tier programs in sciences, humanities, and social sciences, it prepares students for careers in academia, research, and beyond. ENS offers an intellectually stimulating environment, exceptional faculty, and interdisciplinary flexibility. While the workload is demanding, the rewards are immense for those passionate about learning and discovery.

name: École Normale Supérieure (Paris)
text: I would say it’s a great institutions and good for relating and meeting people of diverse culture, it was a good experience and helps in leaning different languages especially English and French I highly recommend the institution

name: École Normale Supérieure (Paris)
text: ENS allows its students to start a strongly research-based and customized study program. The low number of students and the high number of professors allows for a close relationship between them, which is the starting point for a successful career in Science or Letter. Despite some of the courses can be not aligned with the expectations, the wide range of options proposed meets people’s needs. Moreover, a big advantage of this formation is the fact that students can follow courses in apparently totally distinct areas of knowledge spanning from mathematics to cinema. Outside courses, ENS offers a nice familiar environment rich in cultural events organized from students to students. Moreover lots of clubs, there, group people sharing a passion for art, for robotics or whatever. What is important to have clear in mind is that ENS is still naive concerning internationalization: lots of students arrive from all over the world for exchanges but it’s still hard for them to be involved in the life of home students. I think that this is the area in which more can be done and must be done.

name: HEC Paris
text: HEC Paris has been a fantastic experience for me, going much beyond academics! The professors are great mentors who walk us through cutting-edge principles and practical applications. We’re talking about innovative ideas, industrial partnerships, and global opportunities that can help us prepare for a global future.HEC Paris achieved the ideal balance between tradition and innovation. HEC Paris strikes the optimal blend between demanding academics and a supportive community. The instructors are excited about what they teach and are always willing to help, ensuring that you do not just study but also grasp the content.

name: HEC Paris
text: I had an incredible experience at HEC Paris. The classes are interesting, the lecturers are excellent, and I believe I am gaining a useful education. This institution’s people and faculty are outstanding, and while here, I discovered my actual self. I always feel comfortable on campus because it is the safest place in town. The students at HEC Paris are unquestionably its greatest asset.All of the students are determined to succeed and have a strong passion for a variety of activities. The level of diversity you encounter is extraordinary.

name: HEC Paris
text: I chose HEC Paris for its strong sense of community. While other colleges offered identical academic degrees, HEC Paris’ pleasant culture and helpful environment really spoke to me. The tough courses, competent professors, and extensive career preparation have given me a well-rounded educational experience. I would not exchange my experience at HEC Paris for anything. Given their commitment and energy, I believe they are eager to learn and engage in class. I am confident that my time at HEC Paris will prepare me for a great future, and I am pleased to be a part of it.

name: HEC Paris
text: One of the topics I’d want to discuss the most is our teachers’ desire to go above and beyond to assist us achieve. Whether it’s through office hours, online correspondence, or staying after class, they are always available to answer our inquiries and offer helpful advice. HEC Paris’ commitment to our academic development is very outstanding. They foster a pleasant learning environment in which students feel comfortable asking for assistance and sharing their views and ideas.This not only promotes a sense of belonging and community, but it also allows us to learn from our peers and participate in meaningful discussions.

name: HEC Paris
text: Programs are generally tought in English and have Strong academic foundation and access to practical business knowledge. Located in the center of Paris with good environment and professors. But the administration is very slow and annoying. And secondly if your financial situation is not strong than i don’t think it will be a smart choice.

name: Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1
text: The University of Lyon 1 is very amazing, if you are talking about science and technology, UCBL is among the best universities in france. It has good facilities, the library is well equipped and the environment was super conducive. The University is very closed to different supermarkets. The professors are very good and they Master their subjects except one or two of them that we find it difficult to understand him well because of the language, the program I studied (MSc in Synthesis Catalysis and Sustainable Chemistry) was in English, and his English was not good enough. As an English speaking student, it is difficult to have new friends if you can’t speak French fluently.

name: Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1
text: The university itself is a bit outdated and the atmosphere can be quite serious. It can be difficult to enjoy the social aspect of university life when you’re focused on a demanding academic program. However, it’s important to remember that there is a diverse group of students at the university, with a variety of backgrounds and interests. It’s possible to find a niche and connect with like-minded people, even in a more traditional academic environment.

name: Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1
text: The university is a bit old and the people you will find in it aren’t always very funny. I think they are a lot of different people in it and it’s difficult to appreciate the student life with a very strong programme

name: Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1
text: I’ve done my Master-2 degree in this university in SCSC ( synthesis, catalysis, and sustainable chemistry) program. It was a tough program with a majority of organic chemistry where anyone (if he is willing to improve himself) can get a ton of help from all doctors specially the program director. The program also consists of catalytic courses in the second priority, further more the program emphasize on the green routes and cleaner chemical strategies, as it is being the future of chemistry.

name: Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1
text: Being graduated as a master’s student from the Claude Bernard lyon 1 university I can properly argue that it is one of the best universities in France. It has several branches of differemt majors of studies with trained and professional doctors. The international master courses were really interesting and sometimes challenging.The only challenge was to get an internship which was not as easy as expected.

name: École Polytechnique
text: It is a great university, highly regarded in the STEM world. There are many opportunities we can enjoy during out time there, including research projects or student life involvements. The only downside is the campus which is a bit old.

name: École Polytechnique
text: TL;DR – Despite a heavy workload, the BX program allows you to meet amazing people as well as gain incredible amounts of knowledge and experience while being an amazing launchpad for graduate education.

name: École Polytechnique
text: 10/10 I took the Math/CS double major, and I’m now a PhD student at MIT. The program has insane master’s/phd placements internationally. Very few ppl work directly afterward.

name: École Polytechnique
text: It was one of the best experiences of my life, with very strong opportunities for development on academic, personal and professional levels. Within the Masters community, the student body is very diverse and caring for one another. However, some courses could be taught in a more dynamic way, especially the scientific classes.

name: École Polytechnique
text: Overall really good program, small classes that allows you to know more or less everyone in your promn and top notch professors. Except the poor location of the University, the campus was quite lively (thanks to the effort of the bachelors’ students associations). However, be prepared to have problems with the administration which can be quite slow…

name: École Polytechnique
text: – Useful courses – Good student life – Great exchange semester – Awesome offers at the end – International cohort – Palaiseau is not a very fun place though, good if you can go to Paris on the weekend

name: École Polytechnique
text: The bachelor of Polytechnique is a rigorous program with a high workload, excellent professors, and brilliant students. The courses are extremely interesting and allow us to explore a variety of fields before specializing. The program is heavily focused on mathematics, which I particularly enjoy. While the courses are very challenging, the professors and teaching assistants are always happy to help, and there is a lot of collaboration between students. The student body is very diverse and highly inclusive.

name: École Polytechnique
text: Campus life is amazing, with everyone living in the same building, you can do anything with anyone at anytime. Courses are very good quality, math level very advanced compared to similar universities. Professors are very nice, easily find an Research Assistantship.

name: École Polytechnique
text: Academic Opportunities: Building Connections and Research Experience

name: École Polytechnique
text: I am studying for the Masters in Data Science for Business which is a combined program by HEC Paris and Ecole Polytechnique – two of the most prestigious schools in France. The program is highly dynamic, the courses are challenging so you are always on your toes and the professors are incredibly knowledgeable in their field. It’s a bit theoretical in the beginning as classes focus on the mathematical foundations of Data science but overall, it’s a great program. Finding internships could be a bit difficult if you do not speak French as many companies require you to have a certain fluency but it’s not impossible. Regarding campus life, it is extremely beautiful with a lake, arcade, swimming pool, cafeteria, bar, music rooms and much more. There is always something happening on campus so you will be entertained throughout the year!

name: École Polytechnique
text: The university is very welcoming, and everything is very clear. The course is fantastic, since it’s focused on providing real experience during the studies. The professors are good but the TAs are a bit bad. The level of difficulty is very achievable and it doesn’t take much work

name: École Polytechnique
text: EPFL is an exceptional university that embodies excellence in education and research. With its world-class faculty, collaborative environment, and cutting-edge technology, it empowers students to thrive and innovate. Its commitment to pushing boundaries and tackling global challenges makes EPFL a true pioneer in shaping the future.

name: École Polytechnique
text: It is a great university, well ranked worldwide, with a beautiful view on the lac and the Alpes from the campus. The courses and the programs are really interesting, the teachers are smart, but you have to work a lot, specially during the first year of bachelor, which is a kind of selective year.

name: École Polytechnique
text: The campus is driven by the leading class research and innovation mindset. I really liked how you are surrounded by people from a variety of background and this school sets a new standard of quality in education. The cost of the school is funded in large proportion by the Swiss government, making it really affordable for students. I would criticize the quality of the teaching. All professors are researcher, and some of them don’t have an education background at all. It makes it really difficult sometimes to get it through. Thankfully we are surrounded by older students and teaching assistants that can help us.

name: Université Paris-Saclay
text: Université Paris-Saclay offers a world-class educational experience, combining cutting-edge research, supportive faculty, and a dynamic international environment. The modern campus and strong ties to Paris enhance both academic and professional opportunities. It is ideal for students seeking innovation and global connections. I highly recommend for those aiming to excel in their field.

name: Université Paris-Saclay
text: Université Paris-Saclay is undoubtedly one of the best places to study science and engineering in France. Access to cutting-edge labs and world-renowned researchers is an incredible asset. The teachers are very approachable and the program is intensive but very rewarding. However, the campus is a bit isolated, which can limit social life options outside of class. Despite this, the research opportunities are incredible, and students are well prepared for an international career. For science enthusiasts, this is a university that I highly recommend.

name: Université Paris-Saclay
text: At Université Paris-Saclay, students benefit from high-quality education and close connections to the research sector. The campus is modern and well-equipped, with state-of-the-art research facilities. The quality of the courses is excellent, and the university attracts talented individuals from around the world, creating a uniquely diverse learning environment. However, since the campus is located on the outskirts, commuting can be challenging for some students. Despite this, the university offers numerous student associations, making it easy to build a strong network. Paris-Saclay is an ideal place for those looking to pursue advanced research and a career in science.

name: Université Toulouse III Paul Sabatier
text: I really like it, great facilities, huge spaces, €1 restaurants, student accommodation nearby which is very convenient for everyday life, the location is a bit far from the city centre but thanks to the metro it’s not a problem

name: Université Toulouse III Paul Sabatier
text: Very good, huge facilities, very convenient €1 restaurant, great location And passionate teachers, which makes everyday life very pleasant. Good student life

name: Université Toulouse III Paul Sabatier
text: I’m studying medicine and the university is pretty good. We have affordable student accommodation, and a restaurant where you can get everything for 1 euro, which is very convenient on a daily basis. The infrastructure is immense, even if it is a bit old too

name: Université Toulouse III Paul Sabatier
text: The professors are incredible, dedicated, and friendly. The courses offered are diverse and varied, and the instruction is comprehensive and informative. While some parts of the university appear new and well-maintained (the green spaces in particular), some classrooms and buildings could definitely use a facelift.

name: Université Toulouse III Paul Sabatier
text: My school is one of a kind. Its beauty is matched only by its lush green gardens, which add to the splendor of its exterior. The teachers are truly exceptional, making learning easy and always keeping things fascinating. The staff are simply fantastic, approachable and always willing to lend a helping hand. I love my school and would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a good education from a reputable school.

name: University of Birmingham
text: I really like University of Birmingham and find it quite unique compared to other universities. During the summer break, the university hosts a week full of free activities and events to enjoy post exam season and also provides opportunities for growth . Also, the medicine course is extremely organised and nicely structured allowing us to gain experience in GP placements alongside learning content in lectures and small group sessions. It has allowed me to become more confident and improve in my communication. Overall, I would definitely recommend University of Birmingham as a university to attend as it provides many great opportunities for academic and personal growth.

name: University of Birmingham
text: The MSc at Birmingham is genuinely rewarding. The course is structured around real interaction, live client projects, and hands-on experience that actually prepares you for the industry. The professors are incredibly approachable, always happy to revisit topics in class and available after sessions if you have questions, whether about the subject or about navigating life as an international student. Birmingham itself is the second largest city in the UK, which means you have everything you need and as an Indian student you feel right at home from day one. The campus is beautiful and lively with over 20,000 students, and the range of courses on offer is impressive. I would absolutely recommend the University of Birmingham to any international student considering postgraduate study in the UK.

name: University of Birmingham
text: As a B.A. International Relations student at the University of Birmingham (UoB), I can confidently say that choosing to enrol here was a great decision and one I do not regret. UoB does not just concern itself with students’ academic lives, but also places real importance on social life and overall student experience. There are many services available that support future job opportunities; including CV writing support and work experience schemes, which I have personally found very useful.

name: University of Birmingham
text: I’ve absolutely loved my time at the University of Birmingham and would highly recommend it to prospective students. One of the biggest highlights for me has been the quality of teaching. My lecturers are experts in their fields and are genuinely passionate about their subjects. In my Business Management with Marketing course, classes are engaging and practical where we don’t just learn theory, we apply it through real-world case studies. The campus itself is another standout feature. It’s beautiful. The main library facilities are excellent, especially during exam season when having reliable study spaces makes a huge difference. Student life is vibrant and diverse. There are so many societies and events that it’s easy to meet people and get involved. Birmingham as a city is also a big plus. If I had to mention a downside, some modules can feel quite content-heavy at times, so good time management is essential. However, this is manageable and part of the challenge of studying at a respected university. Overall, my experience has been incredibly positive. I’ve grown academically, professionally, and personally during my time here. I would definitely recommend the University of Birmingham to students looking for strong academics, an active campus life, and a supportive learning environment.

name: University of Birmingham
text: An excellent, student oriented university which takes interest in its students to the fullest and keeps in touch with them years after graduation.

name: University of Birmingham
text: I love the campus and feel right at home, there is such a wide variety of modules and opportunities to volunteer and further my studies.
author: Abigail Hunt, Geography

name: University of Birmingham
text: I've had an amazing time studying at the University of Birmingham. I'm now in my final year, having undertaken a placement year last year. The extracurricular and career opportunities have shaped my experience the most and helped me land my dream graduate role.
author: EA, Business Management with Year in Industry

name: University of Birmingham
text: One of the best aspects of the university is that it combines city life with the safety and community feel of a campus. Socially, there is a wide range of options, from nightlife to societies and activities for those who prefer quieter, non-drinking events.
author: ET, Psychology

name: University of Birmingham
text: I love the community and diversity here, even far from home, I feel like I can find food and people that I grew up with. The campus itself is gorgeous red-brick and I feel like the facilities are so nice.
author: NS, Law LLB

name: University of Birmingham
text: The University of Birmingham is a great place to study, especially for my course, Music. The campus is safe and looks very nice, especially in Spring and Summer.
author: SR, Music BMus

name: University of Birmingham
text: The campus at UoB is really lovely, lots of green space and amazing buildings. The pastoral care and support at UOB is amazing; it's very easy to contact staff and arrange appointments with faculty members.
author: SR, Digital Media and Communications BA

name: University of Birmingham
text: I love Selly Oak, and the community students feel it has. The campus itself is beautiful and a great environment to work in.
author: LP, Geography with Year Abroad

name: University of Bristol
text: Other than having to climb hills every day, there aren’t any other downsides. The people here are all very friendly, it’s not hard to meet the grade requirements, and there are tons of club activities—I joined two of them during my freshman year.

name: University of Bristol
text: my university is a great citadel of knowledge and a place where learning is conducive for those from home and abroad. Relationship with course mates and supervisor is top notch and the environment is somewhere that can be easily navigated

name: University of Bristol
text: The tutors at the University of Bristol are excellent. Most professors take the time to remember my name and they go above and beyond in office hours. The law library is well stocked and the city is vibrant and student friendly. My only critique is that many law research internships are reserved for local students. This feels exclusionary for those of us from elsewhere who also want to gain experience. Overall I would still recommend Bristol for its supportive and intellectually rigorous environment.

name: University of Bristol
text: Amidst the bustling student life on (and off) campus, deeply engaging studies, and the buzz of beginning a new chapter in your life, it can be easy to feel overwhelmed at the start of your university experience. However, what particularly stands out to me about UoB is the accessibility of information that staff members and various organisations within the university provide. Staff are always offering to provide guidance where necessary and there are a multitude of organisations to help students in regards to future employment, mental and physical health conditions, and general wellbeing. University of Bristol builds futures for its students and their efforts make a lasting impact on students.

name: University of Bristol
text: The University of Bristol strikes a strong balance between the social and academic aspects of university life. The Students’ Union and student services regularly share events and activities that help create a supportive environment for study, while individual departments organise initiatives that bridge the gap between academic subjects and everyday life. In my experience studying History, there are clear links between the course and the city itself, reflecting a wider, university-wide engagement with Bristol’s past. The only factor that has stopped me from giving Bristol a 5-star review is its well-known unreliability with accommodation. I would recommend that you carefully research private and university accommodation, and be prepared to be flexible/make sacrifices. Ultimately, there is no bad place to live, as long as you are in Bristol!

name: University of Bristol
text: Lots of the lectures are really passionate. Worst: Most social activities revolve around drinking.
author: Joshua Nott, Biology

name: University of Bristol
text: Don't do it guys. The most unsupportive university, no wonder we have a mental health crisis. The vet school take in far too many students for them to be able to adequately support any of them.
author: Student, Veterinary Science BVSc

name: University of Bristol
text: The people at the University of Bristol are very welcoming. One thing for improvement is to have better and proper lecture halls.
author: Yuen Ting Tammy Chan, Law LLB

name: University of Bristol
text: Amazing place amazing teachers who are really knowledgeable.
author: Nicky Lu, Law LLB

name: University of Bristol
text: Bristol is a really good city with loads of things to do. Creates a really nice environment to live in.
author: Jasmine, Mathematics and Philosophy

name: University of Bristol
text: Amazing city and nightlife, very social and easy to meet people. Great uni buildings, lots of great study spaces and places to chill with friends.
author: Harriet Newman, Mathematics and Philosophy MSci

name: University of Bristol
text: Lectures are high quality, lecturers are passionate and facilities are good, have access to anything I will have needed.
author: Lee Caine, Mathematics and Philosophy

name: University of Cambridge
text: The university has highly selective, and elite program that delivers massive career acceleration and salary growth. It has intense pacing and strict academic grading making it incredible demanding. The socialization at the university also offers a diverse career growth.

name: University of Cambridge
text: Cambridge was intense but inspiring. Long nights in historic libraries, challenging lectures, and brilliant peers made it unforgettable. I learned a lot, but the workload was relentless. overall pretty good.

name: University of Cambridge
text: As one of the world’s most prestigious universities, Cambridge combines the highest standards of academic rigour with amazing opportunities to grown, learn and develop, and provides a qualification for life.

name: University of Cambridge
text: I like how the process of operation’s are being handled,and the service to students is of best quality.i recommend more and more to enroll with them for best services,more importantly if you really want to be best in your qualification of choice.

name: University of Cambridge
text: I met with so many new, cool and friendly people that really helped to make a difference. I thoroughly enjoyed my time here. It was the very best years of my life. If I could go back again I most likely would as i enjoyed it so much

name: University of Cambridge
text: Best is the facilities and how the uni looks. Worst is the student life.
author: Sian O'connel, History and Politics BA

name: University of Cambridge
text: I wish the lectures were a little later but besides that it really is a great place.
author: Daniel Bacter, English BA

name: University of Cambridge
text: No wonder it's one of the best universities in the world it really is great here.
author: James Graham, Philosophy BA

name: University of Cambridge
text: I wish the campus was closer to the station and that more support was provided.
author: Eleanor Ruggers, Law BA

name: University of Cambridge
text: Everything is wonderful here, it is exactly how I imagined.
author: Rajesh Nadeem, Engineering MEng

name: University of Cambridge
text: Best part is the experience of living in the historic city- feeling a part of something. The night life isn't great but it's fun with friends.
author: Hannah Bradley, History BA

name: University of Cambridge
text: Best resources and opportunities, but poor care about welfare.
author: Brian Lee, Veterinary Medicine MB

name: University of Cambridge
text: An intense experience all round.
author: Florence, Land Economy BA

name: University of Edinburgh
text: I enjoyed my time a the university deeply. Yet, I didn’t enjoy it as much as I should have, both in terms of academic and social lives. It’s one of the top universities, yet it’s highly disorganized in some unexplainable manner. So, if you don’t know how to use your time and resources and make the best of it, you’ll miss out on everything. I guess it’s similar in other places, but Edinburgh University really is the best, but nobody directs you towards getting that greatness. It’s also in the most beautiful city in the world.

name: University of Edinburgh
text: My experience at the University of Edinburgh so far has been a very positive one. As a second-year History student, I find the biggest selling point of studying here is the privilege of living centrally in the city itself.

name: University of Edinburgh
text: The university is based in a beautiful city providing a wonderful, rich cultural experience. It can be expensive and it is a little limited on ethnic diversity though; which is a shame. As the university itself it is resting on prestige without putting in imput to drive improvements in the student experience. My programme fell short of expectations, and I wouldn’t recommend it.

name: University of Edinburgh
text: I’ve had a fantastic time at the University of Edinburgh. I’ve made lifetime friends, experienced great memories, and witnessed considerable progress in my studies. The lectures are entertaining and informative, making the coursework more pleasant and understandable. I am grateful to be a member of such a lovely community. The university provides tremendous chances, from joining clubs to finding internships, and the campus is beautiful. Being here has had a significant good impact on my mental health, and I am grateful for the opportunity to study at such an incredible university.

name: University of Edinburgh
text: I have a high regard for the University of Edinburgh, where professors are not just specialists in their subjects, but also enthusiastic about educating and helping students. Their dedication to our academic advancement is clear in their entertaining lectures and discussions. The university’s demanding workload has substantially aided my intellectual development, and the faculty is extremely educated and encouraging. I particularly like the University of Edinburgh’s varied and inclusive culture, which fosters a positive and friendly environment for all students. The staff are personable, and the university’s emphasis on diversity is absolutely admirable.

name: University of Edinburgh
text: Cool libraries, lots of societies. Bad support for mental health.
author: Chris Howat, Celtic and Linguistics MA

name: University of Edinburgh
text: I love the city. Edinburgh is big enough have the cosmopolitan element of a city, but small enough that it is not as overwhelming as most cities.
author: Anais, Japanese MA

name: University of Edinburgh
text: Edinburgh is an open minded and welcoming city. Most people you encounter at the University of Edinburgh will be willing to help you out and get settled.
author: David Cisneros, Business with Marketing MA

name: University of Edinburgh
text: Amazing funding and facilities for my course but the range of student diversity and student support is a bit lacking.
author: Jasmine Greener, Archaeology and Social Anthropology MA

name: University of Edinburgh
text: There is not enough on campus study space. The Principal is paid too much.
author: Elinor, English Literature MA

name: University of Edinburgh
text: Good city, easy access to everything.
author: Maria Joseph, Chemical Engineering BEng

name: Imperial College London
text: Proud to be making history at such an highly esteemed institution. Imperial College London, deserves it’s rank in the top 10. The level of organization and professionalism by staff members is in a league of it’s own.

name: Imperial College London
text: Imperial College London is the most impressive higher institution i have ever been around, their research is at a top level and the whole organization is just extremely impressive. They offer different scholarships to individuals from different backgrounds, making it accessible to the common man.

name: Imperial College London
text: The University included different subjects which suitable for different person. Professors are nice and professional. When You have any questions, they are always ready to help and discuss with you. You can enjoy the study environment, the facilities in campus is in very good condition.

name: Imperial College London
text: Great resources, great lecturers and lots of skills gained. The campus is in the heart of London and provides great job opportunities in many fields. In addition, there are loads of societies catering to everyone’s interests

name: Imperial College London
text: Imperial is tough. It will work you hard; work hard, play harder. Imperial sets you up for life, from skills to networking opportunities from peers to faculty to job prospects. With more societies than any other university Imperial boasts a home for you.

name: Imperial College London
text: The university is great to study at with lots of events and societies to attend. The teaching is excellent however the course is quite intense meaning you have to spend a lot of time studying leaving less time to socialise.
author: Reece Carter, Mathematics BSc

name: Imperial College London
text: Excellent course with great tutors and a supportive faculty. The course is blended, including clinical contact from year one, and the teaching is very thorough and relevant. Clinical skills training also begins from term 1.
author: Natalie, Medicine MB BS

name: Imperial College London
text: A very good university with lots of great job prospects. The course can be very tough sometimes, but it's well worth it in the end. Management modules only start in 3rd year.
author: Caitlin, Electrical & Electronic Engineering with Management MEng

name: Imperial College London
text: I had a great time. The social life is ok but nothing special as a lot of the kids here are very studious and only focus on their degree. So many extra curricular opportunities with hundreds of societies to join and do scuba or climbing or whatever. Even a cheese society. But if you meet the right people it doesn't matter because you're in London.
author: Charlie, Chemistry MSci

name: Imperial College London
text: Has been taught mostly online but been a good experience. Good facilities and good management for COVID but online provisions for labs has not been very good as they haven't been interactive. Excellent support from staff.
author: B, Chemistry with Spanish for Science MSci

name: Imperial College London
text: Overall experience in Imperial College London has been mediocre. The people definitely made the study life better but in terms of education, Imperial has a lot more work to do. In particular from a student well being and welfare point of view.
author: Gary Tan, Biochemistry BSc

name: Imperial College London
text: Accommodation was good value but not maintained well enough; very good nightlife in the city; Facilities were pretty good; Really good range of clubs; Did not use any of the support services; very good job prospects.
author: Student, Aeronautical Engineering MEng

name: Imperial College London
text: The college is great, location is amazing. I studied in the physics department. The work ethic is strong, so if you want to coast I wouldn't recommend here. The tutors are top notch and unbelievably clever and friendly, no question is too stupid and they're always happy to speak to you. The only reason I wouldn't give it 5 stars is, like most places I assume, the admin are terrible. Like really annoying.
author: Student, Physics MSci

name: Imperial College London
text: Imperial College London is a relatively good college with many clubs and societies, but not very good for Chemistry. London has a lot of opportunities and activities. The job prospects are excellent because of the name of Imperial.
author: Student, Chemistry with Research Abroad and a Year in Industry MSci

name: King's College London
text: King’s College London offers a strong academic environment with knowledgeable lecturers and a diverse student community. Its central London location provides excellent access to cultural attractions, internships, and career opportunities, making it an exciting place to study. The university has modern facilities, a wide range of student societies, and good support services that help students settle into university life.

name: King's College London
text: Versatile degree with many potential teaching pathways and career options. However a degree focused a lot on social studies rather than culture and media. There should be more of a focus on this given the degree title, making it seem a bit misleading.

name: King's College London
text: I really enjoyed my time at King’s College London. I liked the supportive professors, interesting courses, and the diverse international community. Sometimes administrative processes were a bit slow, but overall the experience was great. I would definitely recommend it to other students.

name: King's College London
text: What an amazing college. Love the faculty and insustry exposure we get here. The beamst part is that this is the experience across all disciplines. Great location and environment which makes studying here a great fun experience as well.

name: King's College London
text: As a Master of Science student in International Marketing at King’s College London, I have thoroughly enjoyed my time at the university. I particularly appreciated the strong balance between theory and practical application, which allowed me to develop both academic insight and real-world skills. Modules such as Consumer Behaviour and Marketing Theory & Practice were especially valuable, providing deep understanding of how customers make decisions and how to design effective marketing strategies. The programme also encouraged collaboration and critical thinking, fostering a supportive environment where I could engage with peers from diverse backgrounds. Overall, I would highly recommend King’s College London to prospective marketing students seeking a rigorous, engaging and internationally focused programme.

name: King's College London
text: In a great location in the heart of London, so much to do and see. A good mix of online and in person classes.
author: Fatima Kamran, Mental Health Nursing BSc

name: King's College London
text: Not very good support for mental health, you can feel isolated quite quickly in the big city.
author: Holly Jàck, Law LLB

name: King's College London
text: Lots of opportunities, events and societies.
author: Duyanh, Business Management BSc

name: King's College London
text: It is located within the heart of London which allows us to explore the city, and the student societies are my favourite.
author: Maryam Alsbach, Adult Nursing BSc

name: King's College London
text: It provides many opportunities and it is a very nice campus.
author: Elena Sini, Biochemistry BSc

name: King's College London
text: The best aspect of uni is the support given, especially as a first year where everything is new and overwhelming.
author: Devorah Grunwald, Midwifery BSc

name: King's College London
text: It's a great place to study, provides all the facilities needed and amazing tutors.
author: Filsa Ahmed, Midwifery BSc

name: London School of Economics
text: The college offers a marketable course with high standards. The college offers good training marking graduates marketable and highly valued in work areas . the university ensures good discipline is cultivated as a culture producing disciplined individuals

name: London School of Economics
text: Great university. I really enjoyed my master degree in HRM. There was also an option for part-time studies, so I picked this one, and was able to complete it alongside my job. The teaching staff were all excellent, with many coming from private sector background, then doing Phd and now doing teaching.

name: London School of Economics
text: What would you highlight? The university’s outstanding academic reputation, expert faculty, and exceptional research opportunities.

name: London School of Economics
text: It is a good environment for learning and development. and you will get great experience and will feel loved .It is an edifice full of respect, cooperation, love, development and determination.It is a very good experience and a unique and distinguished place with an atmosphere of friendship, love and flexibility.

name: London School of Economics
text: I really enjoyed my time at LSE, the diversity in the range of modules and the level of expertise from the staff enhanced my degree and personal interests. One thing that I disliked, however, was the University’s and the student body’s intensive focus on careers, especially the corporate world. This was a bit suffocating for me, due to the fact that I have no interest in this field. I wish there would be more awareness on the diversity of jobs available.

name: London School of Economics
text: I really like my university and course – PBS. We have a range of ways of assessment including pitches, presentations, podcasts. There is a great sense of community as we are not in competition with each other.

name: London School of Economics
text: LSE is a great place to create life-long connections and friends. There’s a certain sense of community that is special to the university. The staff are hands-on and approachable. The facilities are modern and well-equipped.

name: London School of Economics
text: Pros: Networking opportunities, academically challenging programme (intensive 1 year)

name: London School of Economics
text: The course I picked, BSc Financial Mathematics and Statistics is one of the most demanding courses at LSE, but is worth it, as the work you put in ultimately translates to your results. You do have leisure time to go out and spend time with friends as well as studying. The work ethic at LSE is overall very strong, with people pushing each other to work as hard as they can.

name: London School of Economics
text: One of the most notable aspects as a student at LSE is its inspiring work culture. The vast cultural diversity that exists between the university walls are home to driven and motivated individuals from all over the globe. Such a multicultural atmosphere fosters a rich exchange of ideas, enabling students to broaden their horizons. This coupled with the school’s emphasis on collaboration and critical thinking nurtures an intellectually stimulating environment where students are constantly learning from one another and their experiences. I have found that the dedication from the career services provides comprehensive and supportive guidance throughout each student’s academic journey. From personalised career counselling sessions to skill-building workshops and networking events loaded with leading industry professionals and alumni, LSE equips its students with the necessary tools to successfully navigate the competitive job market. RIghtly renowned for its academic excellence, LSE attracts top-tier faculty members who are not only highly knowledgeable but also passionate about their subjects, creating engaging learning experiences as I have found while studying my course. My lecturers are incredibly accomplished and passionate about their fields of study without which I would not be as immersed in my studies as I am. It is important to note that the London School of Economics lives up to its academic prestige which comes with a level of demand from students that are only compatible with immense determination and stellar time management, especially from those who would also like to pursue extracurricular activities. That being said, forming a community with students who have similar interests and aspirations as you is the key to ensuring that you are able to remain on track with your goals. Overall I would recommend LSE for those who value and aspire to be in a hardworking environment.

author: Student, Management BSc

name: London School of Economics
text: The events carried out by the various clubs are plenty and very interesting.
author: Student, Actuarial Science BSc

name: University of Manchester
text: Manchester is one of those universities that doesn’t just teach you a degree, it exposes you to a world. Studying Fashion Management here meant being surrounded by people from every corner of the globe, which pushed me creatively and professionally in ways I didn’t expect.

name: University of Manchester
text: i was there a few years ago, tutors were fantastic, and they helped me progress in life. but the best thing about this place is the lifestyle you lead, hormones running high and you can only imagine the fun we had with friends on nights out, great experience

name: University of Manchester
text: Great online/distance learning programmes. Lecturers respond quickly, have materials developed specifically for the online version of the course, and there’s lots of engagement with other students. Would recommend!

name: University of Manchester
text: It was, hopefully still is, a great university and lived up to its reputation both for learning and for living the student life. I was there 1975 to 1979 and did a Maths degree followed by an MSc in Numerical Analysis and Conputing – stood me well in my career. I suspect none or my lecturers are still there! And sadly they demolished the Maths Tower as well.

name: University of Manchester
text: Fantastic to take a course at this great university. Fantastic experience taking a course at this great university. The teaching is clear and engaging, the support is strong, and the learning environment feels inspiring, welcoming, and motivating every single day.

name: University of Manchester
text: It feels closer to what most people would call an “easy and quick” registration process — especially compared to universities that require in-person steps. But “quickness” depends a lot on whether payment/fees, visa (if relevant), and documentation are ready when you begin registration. The online registration journey is easy and quick.

name: University of Manchester
text: When an archaic old timely red-brick campus is mixed with the inventive and experimental fashion and music style of students in Manchester, one cannot help but watch in awe. This industrial town is a hotspot of exploration, friendship and academic endeavours. The university life is like no other. Students from other universities posthumously recognise Manchester students as undeniably cool. The DJ scene and accessibility to music venues in the city is a privilege like no other. The welcoming nature of students and the interconnectivity between friends is only found at the University of Manchester. The clubs and societies available are held in high esteem, such as the Mancunion and the various sports on offer. The lecturers are world famous and clearly have a passion for their subjects. The facilities are modern and ergonomically designed. The student housing is bustling and thriving with people excited for their next stage in life. Manchester university and the city of Manchester is somewhere I have absolutely fallen in love with and will have no doubt that anyone who comes here will fall in love with it too.

name: University of Manchester
text: My experience at the University of Manchester was largely positive and academically enriching. I appreciated the university’s strong emphasis on research and critical thinking, which challenged me to deepen my understanding and develop practical skills relevant to my field. The diversity of the student body and the vibrant campus life helped foster a supportive and inclusive environment. Access to excellent resources, knowledgeable faculty, and a network of peers also enhanced my learning experience.

name: University of Manchester
text: Dreadful course. If you want to do endless, pointless lab reports on water flowing through pipes, or be judged on the aptitude of your group rather than your own, or not have any recourse against GTAs who mark your work, then this is the university for you.
author: Student, Chemical Engineering

name: University of Manchester
text: Everything on campus appealing and accessible, more study spaces since they can be very busy.
author: Kyle Russell, Computer Science and Mathematics with Industrial Experience

name: University of Oxford
text: I really enjoyed my time at Oxford. The teaching style suited me: it was a few small group/1:1/1:2 sessions a week. Most of my time was spent reading in a library, following threads of my own interest. This did not suit everyone – a few of my friends really struggled with the lack of contact and structure, but I enjoyed the close relationship to my tutors and the ability to focus on things that genuinely excited me. The opportunity to take part in a lot of extracurricular activities – from sports to art to debating – was also very enjoyable and unique. Plus, the city is just gorgeous! You can really romanticise your life as a stressed, overworked student when you get to walk around a place that literally looks like Hogwarts. Your experience will depend on the specific college you go to – so make sure to research that.

name: University of Oxford
text: The architecture is out of this world; the Victorian buildings are rich in history, and it’s like stepping back into the 60s. The university is clean and safe; I would recommend it. The professors helped me catch up when I was falling behind; the classes are breathtaking. I didn’t regret my choice of studying abroad instead of sticking to South African universities.

They are always patient and they explain things step by step till you get help and also they always contact you as early as possible once they receive your inquiry but it is always best to go in person for a better experience and to ensure that all your concerns are dealt with.

name: University College London
text: I studied at UCL for a year and graduated successfully. I studied Information Science. All the staff and students were incredibly friendly. UCL is located in the heart of London. It is a city with a rich history and a beautiful atmosphere.

name: University College London
text: University College London (UCL) is widely regarded as one of the top universities in London, and my experience there reflects its strong reputation. The university offers a diverse and intellectually stimulating environment, attracting students from all over the world. This diversity enriches both academic and social life, making it a truly global institution. Academically, UCL maintains high standards. The lecturers are knowledgeable, approachable, and often leaders in their respective fields. Courses are well-structured and encourage critical thinking, independent research, and practical application of knowledge. The university also provides excellent facilities, including modern libraries, research centers, and study spaces that support student learning. One of the standout aspects of UCL is its central London location. Being in the heart of the city provides easy access to cultural landmarks, professional opportunities, and networking events. This is especially beneficial for students looking to gain work experience alongside their studies. However, like many London universities, the cost of living can be quite high, and accommodation can be competitive. Despite this, the overall experience is rewarding, and the opportunities available often outweigh these challenges. In conclusion, UCL is an excellent choice for students seeking a high-quality education in a vibrant and diverse environment. It offers strong academic support, global exposure, and valuable career opportunities.

name: University College London
text: I completed my MSc at UCL after doing a physics minor during my undergrad studies. The transition into data science was smooth because the curriculum is highly analytical, heavy on statistical modeling, and provides a rock-solid foundation in mathematical methods. You get deep, hands-on programming experience using Python and SQL for building complex data pipelines and handling massive datasets, which directly maps to industry needs. The career services team has excellent connections within major tech firms and financial institutions right here in London, making it an ideal choice if you want to jump straight into production-level data roles after graduation.

name: University College London
text: I studied at the School of Pharmacy, it was a great university with great supportive staff. It helped shape me as a pharmacist and gave me great foundation to start my career, which I was later able to develop further. I would highly recommend the university, it is in a great location in London.

name: University College London
text: I am currently studying BSc Social Sciences at University College London, and I have found the degree especially valuable for its interdisciplinary approach. The course has allowed me to explore sociology, psychology, politics, economics, research methods, and data analysis, which has helped me develop a broad understanding of how society functions from multiple perspectives. I have particularly appreciated gaining experience in both qualitative and quantitative research, including SPSS and data interpretation, as these skills feel highly transferable beyond academia.

name: University of Galway
text: Studying for a bachelor’s degree at the University of Galway is a rewarding experience that combines strong academic foundations with a vibrant campus life. Beyond academics, life at Galway offers a rich cultural and social experience. The campus is lively and diverse, with students from all over the world, creating an inclusive and international atmosphere. There are numerous clubs, societies, and extracurricular activities that help students build friendships and develop leadership skills. The location in Galway city also adds to the experience, offering a unique blend of tradition, music, and coastal beauty, making student life both enjoyable and memorable.

name: Bocconi University
text: The MSc in Management & Tourism Economics at Bocconi gave me a solid foundation in both strategic management and the economics of service industries. The programme is demanding and fast-paced, which pushes you to develop both analytical and interpersonal skills quickly. Professors bring a good mix of academic rigor and real-world perspective, and the international environment — with students from across Europe and beyond — adds genuine value to classroom discussions. Milan itself is an asset: the proximity to major companies and industry events creates networking opportunities that are hard to replicate elsewhere. If you’re looking for a programme that prepares you for a commercial or consulting career in a competitive environment, Bocconi delivers.

text: The building is really big and sometimes it is difficult to find the classes. Apart from that, I find Polito a very valid study address, because of the formation it gives you. You must study very hard but I recommend.

name: Politecnico di Torino
text: Finishing the university is quite difficult. It’s true that the University has a great reputation and the graduates will benefit from it. However, i do not believe the difficulty makes sense for a bachelor degree.

name: Politecnico di Torino
text: Polito has several campus across the city. Each of them is in a convinient location and close to the bus, trum and metro. Going to the campus is easy because of the location and the transportation system.

name: Sapienza University of Rome
text: Studying the Master’s programme in Transport Systems Engineering at Sapienza University of Rome has been a very positive experience. The course offers a good balance between theory and practical applications, covering modern transport planning, mobility, and infrastructure challenges. The professors are knowledgeable and supportive, and the international environment encourages collaboration with students from different backgrounds.
